title: Senior Executive, Trade Pricing (Shipping Liner MNC)
description: The Client: Our client is a prominent and growing global shipping carrier, known for its excellence in providing reliable and efficient transportation solutions. With a strong presence in both domestic and international markets, they offer dynamic career opportunities within a fast-paced and innovative industry, making it an exciting business to be part of. Job Responsibility: · Analyse pricing strategies at different geographical and trade levels, applying these to ensure trade lane efficiencies and reduction of cost control. · Make key decisions on cost structures and pricing strategies to align with market trends and the company’s objectives. · Lead ongoing improvements in cost models to adapt to market changes, optimizing pricing and cargo selection. · Identify opportunities to adjust strategies based on shifting market conditions and competition. · Regularly analyze differences between estimated and actual shipping costs, taking action to correct discrepancies and improve forecasting accuracy. · Periodically assess container equipment availability and performance to inform decisions about balancing, repositioning, and managing costs effectively. Morgan McKinley Pte Ltd Senjoo Khong EA Licence No: 11C5502 EA Registration No.: R23116117

title: Lecturer (NUS College)
description: Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUSCareer Portal Your application will be processed only if you applyvia NUSCareer Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description NUS College is the honours college of the National University of Singapore. Our focus is a rigorous and stimulating interdisciplinary education designed to help students cultivate the necessary skills to work effectively across boundaries, We plan to hire a dedicated individual  to fill a Lecturer’s position.  This is a full-time position that includes opportunities for pedagogical research funding and for promotion to an Associate Professor on the NUS Educator Track. The primary responsibility is to offer our mandatory courses, to which you will be expected to bring your own academic and intellectual expertise. Duties & Responsibilities For a start, you will be expected to offer the following, Computational Problem Solving (NPS2001) The CPS is a common curricular course designed to introduce NUS College students to the fundamental concepts, computational tools and techniques necessary to solve complex problems. The focus is on computer science and information technology, but course objectives are more general in nature, with the goal of developing essential skills for approaching and understanding new technology to critically assess potential benefits and risks. By having a comprehensive understanding of the potential of new technologies, students will be able to formulate a problem, outline potential technological solutions and set up a team to implement the solutions that can help achieve project goals. Impact Experience Project (NEP3001) The Impact Experience (IEx) is a eight-unit capstone course that all NUS College students will have to undertake for two years. Students will work in interdisciplinary teams to collaborate with non-profit partners and innovate future solutions for the benefit of communities and society in Southeast Asia, including Singapore. Each team of 6-7 students is supervised by a lecturer who specializes in the area they are working with. Students would choose to work with lecturers clustered into three broad areas: Science and Technology, Arts and Design, Civic and Community. Applicants from any field representing the Computer Sciences are welcome to apply for this position. We are interested in candidates whose work is cross-disciplinary and can be applied to better understand and tackle social, environmental and technological problems in the Singaporean context. The selected candidate's primary responsibility will be to teach the CPS together with other course instructors to ensure that students have a common curricular experience while allowing for individual instructor creativity in their seminars. Applicants should demonstrate their ability to teach such a course by providing evidence of teaching interdisciplinary computing courses in the university. The other responsibility will be to supervise IEx student teams working on projects that involve computing for social good. Successful candidates are expected to offer 2-3 sections of Computational Problem Solving in the academic year, an elective of their own design, supervise 3-6 IEx teams, and take on a small supervision load of independent study modules. Qualifications Candidates must hold a PhD. Remuneration and benefits are highly competitive on the global research university level. Expatriate benefits may include travel assistance from overseas and limited housing benefits. Appointments begin at the level of Lecturer on the Educator Track. Although the NUS Educator Track is not a tenurable line, it offers a pathway to promotion to Associate Professorship on an open contract (and beyond), as well as opportunities for pedagogical research funding, including overseas research and conference travel. The contract is for three years in the first instance and is renewable subject to good performance. Interested applicants should prepare and submit the following documents when they apply: •    A cover letter, clearly specifying which position they are applying for. •    CV: An abridged CV (5-page maximum), including details on education, employment history, past experience of teaching and supervision, research activities (including publications) and achievements, honours and awards, and service accomplishments. •    Qualifications: Include scanned copies of academic degrees. •    Teaching Portfolio: o    A Teaching Statement that commences with a preface (maximum 300 words) that makes the case for  appointment. This should be a highly distilled summary of key contributions to student learning and, if applicable, educational leadership, guided by your teaching philosophy. The rest of the Statement, of not more than 2,000 words, should outline your teaching philosophy and your educational contribution across multiple dimensions and roles. Please include evidence on how you have impact students' learning  experience and outcomes as well as how you have influenced peers and the community. o    Teaching evaluations and peer review reports, if available. Other evidence of teaching excellence and/or other contributions to education, e.g., curriculum development, pedagogical innovation etc., should also be provided. •    A proposal, of not more than 500 words, describing a prospective syllabus for the target course. •    A statement, of not more than 500 words, highlighting how you plan to contribute to pedagogical innovation and/or advance interdisciplinary honours education at NUSC. •    FOUR referees' letters (one of whom must be the applicant's PhD supervisor). Interviews will normally be conducted online. Only shortlisted applicants will be notified. More information about NUSC and our curriculum can be found on our website: https://nuscollege.nus.edu.sg/ Additional information about Academic Appointments at NUS can be found here: https://www.nus.edu.sg/careers/

title: Research Fellow (Operations Research & Analytics)
description:
Interested applicants are invited to apply directly at the NUS
Career Portal Your application will be processed only if you apply
via NUS
Career Portal We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Job Description Role Summary: Lead and manage multi-stakeholder research projects in operations research, analytics, and optimization, including collaborations with NTUC FairPrice (Plug-in Flow Routing Optimization Engine; Store Friendly replenishment packing) and PSA (data analysis to improve efficiency in the container logistics ecosystem), as well as other industry and academic projects. Drive end-to-end project delivery—from problem framing and model formulation to implementation, evaluation, and dissemination—while coordinating milestones and deliverables with collaborators. Key Responsibilities: Act as project manager for assigned projects, developing work plans, timelines, risk registers, and progress reports; ensure on-time delivery of milestones and industry-facing deliverables. Work closely with external collaborators (e.g., NTUC FairPrice, Store Friendly, PSA) and internal stakeholders to define objectives, align expectations, and translate operational needs into rigorous research questions. Design and formulate models for routing, packing, replenishment, scheduling, and network optimization; develop algorithms and decision-support tools using appropriate techniques (e.g., mathematical programming, stochastic optimization, simulation, machine learning). Lead data acquisition, cleaning, integration, and analysis; establish data pipelines and quality assurance processes; conduct experiments, benchmarking, and sensitivity analyses to validate models. Prototype and, where applicable, support pilot deployment of solutions; document methodologies, assumptions, reproducible workflows, and user guides. Prepare high-quality publications, technical reports, and presentations; contribute to dissemination through conferences, seminars, and industry briefings. Guide and mentor PhD students, research assistants, and interns; provide technical supervision, review work outputs, and foster a collaborative research culture. Coordinate compliance with NUS and partner data governance, ethics, and security requirements; manage project documentation and IP considerations in line with agreements. Contribute to grant proposals, project scoping, and resource planning; assist with budgeting and procurement related to project needs. Support broader IORA initiatives, including knowledge sharing, seminar organization, and collaborations across the University and with external partners Requirements PhD in Industrial Engineering/Operations Research/Management Science (or closely related), with a strong research record in stochastic modeling and optimization. Hands-on experience solving real-world routing, packing, replenishment, scheduling, and network optimization problems. Proficiency with large-scale decision methods (e.g., approximate dynamic programming/rollout, primal–dual or penalty controls, Lagrangian decomposition/index policies, queueing/fluid models). Strong programming skills in Python and/or Java (MATLAB a plus), with experience in data pipelines, simulation/digital twins, and reproducible experimentation. Proven project management and industry collaboration experience, delivering milestones and translating operational needs into deployable solutions. Strong communication and mentorship: publications, technical reporting, presentations, and guidance of PhD students/RAs.

title: Senior Principal Data Scientist
description: Get to Know the Team The Fulfilment Tech Family is an integral part of Grab, connecting our wide-ranging marketplaces across Southeast Asia. We develop real-time distributed systems and machine learning solutions to process hundreds of millions of requests daily, ensuring efficient allocation of supply, pricing, and seamless order matching. Our mission: Build reliable and impactful products for driver-partners. Optimize the ability to fulfil consumer needs in any conditions. Develop sustainable marketplaces, balancing user experience and operational costs across stakeholders. We're hiring a Senior Principal Data Scientist to join the Fulfilment team. You're an individual contributor whom will lead strategic optimization initiatives that elevate pricing, dispatch, and supply management strategies. You'll combine in-depth data-driven methodologies with a strategic mindset to drive automation and marketplace efficiency. Get to Know the Role As a Senior Principal Data Scientist, you'll shape and advance cross-system Fulfilment strategies, accounting for how passengers and driver-partners respond to platform interventions. You'll leverage machine learning and operations research methodologies to optimize marketplace-level operational outcomes. Through joint optimization across systems—pricing, dispatch, and supply positioning—you'll design interpretable, adaptable, and multi-objective-aware Reinforcement Learning decision agents. You'll model marketplace behaviour and develop policies to automate key aspects of platform operations, contributing lasting impact for customers, driver-partners, and Grab. You'll report to the Head of Data Science and work onsite at Grab's One North Singapore office. The Critical Tasks You Will Perform You will: Develop ML and DL models to improve generalisation and operational efficiency Create unified Reinforcement Learning architectures that coordinate multiple elements, including pricing, dispatch, and supply positioning, while balancing diverse marketplace objectives. Design frameworks for joint optimization of marketplace systems through hierarchical or multi-agent RL approaches that push the operational Pareto frontier. Explore representations of data that consider supply-demand signals, behaviour elasticity, and factors like weather, traffic, and user intent. Build interpretable models to capture passenger and driver decision-making across diverse scenarios and operational conditions. Devise feedback loops to respond to passenger and driver behaviour, considering geographical differences and behavioural patterns over time. Collaborate with data engineers and backend teams to refine and integrate optimization models into real-time production systems. Work with the platform and experimentation teams to validate models in live environments and drive iterative improvements. Guide technical teams by leading efforts in data science while fostering collaboration and excellence. What Essential Skills You Will Need You have a Master Degree in Computer Science, Operations Research, Applied Mathematics, or related field with at least 10 years of relevant experience. You have experience with Reinforcement Learning, MDPs, stochastic control, or behavioural modelling under uncertainty. You have experience deploying ML models in online learning systems, sequential decision frameworks, or simulation-based techniques for optimization tasks. You have fluency in Python and experience with ML libraries such as PyTorch or TensorFlow for developing robust learning algorithms. You are familiar with tools such as Spark or Ray to build scalable workflows for data-driven optimization and training. You can bridge business challenges into concrete modelling problems with actionable outputs. You have advanced skills to interpret platform data and understand marketplace elasticity, user behaviour, and external environmental influences, contributing to actionable insights for real-world applications. You may also apply directly via https://smrtr.io/wRtCm

title: Research Fellow (Aerospace Engineering/Transport/Mechanical Engineering/Computer Science)
description: Air Traffic Management Research Institute (ATMRI) is a research institute at Nanyang Technological University (NTU), Singapore, supported by the Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore (CAAS). ATMRI focuses on developing practical and data-driven solutions for complex aviation systems, in close collaboration with industry and international partners. For more details, please view https://www.ntu.edu.sg/atmri . We are looking for a Research Fellow to conduct research on air traffic management (ATM) algorithms and data-driven methods. The role will focus on developing intelligent methods and operation-support tools for ATM applications, contributing to the advancement of research and innovation at Nanyang Technological University. Key Responsibilities: Conduct research on methods and approaches to support air traffic management studies and applications. Develop, test, and evaluate analytical models, algorithms, and tools using operational and simulated data. Support research activities related to airspace and air traffic operations under current and future ATM concepts. Work collaboratively with researchers and engineers to develop prototype solutions and decision-support tools. Analyse results and assess the performance and applicability of developed methods in realistic operational scenarios. Prepare technical reports, research papers, and project deliverables, and participate in project meetings and reviews. Contribute to research proposals, technical documentation, and other project-related activities as required. Job Requirements: PhD qualification degree in Aerospace Engineering, Transport, Mechanical Engineering, Computer Science, or other related fields. At least three (3) years of relevant research experience in air traffic management or related technical research roles. Familiarity with research related to air traffic management systems, airspace operations, or air traffic operations. Good written and oral communication skills with the ability to prepare technical reports and academic publications. Proficiency in job-specific technical skills, including programming for research and data analysis purposes (e.g. Python or similar languages), as well as strong numerical and analytical skills. Competent in general research-related skills and professional attributes, including problem-solving and analytical thinking. Strong interpersonal skills, with the ability to work independently and collaboratively, develop solutions under defined timelines, and demonstrate good organisational and time management skills. title: Senior (Quantitative Analytics and Treasury Services) - Financial Services FAAS

title: Associate (Quantitative Analytics and Treasury Services) - Financial Services FAAS
description: At EY, you’ll have the chance to build a career as unique as you are, with the global scale, support, inclusive culture and technology to become the best version of you. And we’re counting on your unique voice and perspective to help EY become even better, too. Join us and build an exceptional experience for yourself, and a better working world for all. The opportunity Over the last 20 years, innovative new products have dramatically increased the complexity of the financial markets, from complex preference shares issuance in venture capital world, to structured products allowing clients to repackage and transfer risk, or other more exotic instruments designed to meet almost any goal two parties can agree on. We are looking to grow our Quantitative Analytics and Treasury Services (“QATS”) team within the Financial Services - Financial Accounting Advisory Services (“FAAS”) service line. To be specific, we are looking for valuation and quant finance individuals to support Assurance teams with independent valuation and quantitative analysis on various financial instruments and investments into private enterprises. Included are opportunities to develop individual consulting skillsets and to provide services in model governance and valuation frameworks. The service we provide within EY covers a broad spectrum of responsibilities with respect to financial instruments and business valuation. You will experience ongoing professional development through your interactions with internal and external clients, in conjunction with exposure to various asset classes (e.g. FX, Fixed Income, Equity, Commodity, Interest Rate, Credit, etc.) and industry sectors (e.g. banking, private equity, hedge funds, transportation, commodity, utilities, oil & gas, REITS, e-commerce, fintech, etc). The QATS team at EY uses sophisticated techniques from the field of financial engineering and quantitative finance to assist clients in developing quantitative models for pricing, valuation, risk management and regulatory compliance purposes. Your key responsibilities: Financial Derivatives Valuation and Business Valuation Work together within a team on multiple client engagements in daily independent valuation for a diverse range of financial instruments Assist in developing valuation models to assess a variety of linear and non-linear financial instruments by adopting option pricing theory, fixed income and structured finance models, Monte Carlo simulation, statistical analysis, and other quantitative analysis Perform model validation on derivative pricing models, risk management models and model parameter calibration models Provide assistance in review of fair valuation approach, methodology, and assumptions adopted by private equity and venture capitalist clients Perform portfolio valuation review for private equity, venture capital, and asset management companies with investments Value debt instruments such as straight bonds, convertible bonds, redeemable convertible preference shares Build financial models, such as Option Pricing Model and Binomial Tree Model, to value hybrid instruments Credit Modelling Perform model development and model validations on credit risk models for Basel and IFRS 9 purposes Identify sources of model risk through comprehensive review of all model components and developmental evidence and challenging the assumptions and basis of which the components are being derived Employ statistical modelling methodologies in performing validation and produce results to be analyzed from statistical as well as business perspective. Keep updated on industry developments in terms of regulatory requirements and best practice modelling techniques Write high quality model validation reports and/or presentations Project Management Technically contribute to managing projects which includes packaging overall project findings into clear, concise and high-quality deliverables Ensure all modelling tasks are completed timely Data Analytics Design meaningful application of data analytics to be used for auditing financial numbers including developing toolkit for data mining and work paper documentation standards Use data analytics to provide actionable, timely and accurate data to drive business insights around customer, regulatory risk, compliance, operations and financial to achieve greater return on investment. title: Senior Software Engineer
description: JOB SUMMARY SABRE is Standard Chartered’ s Analytics Booking and Risk Engine and is the strategic risk and PnL platform for Financial Markets. The development team is a world class technology group in Standard Chartered bank to provide technical solutions to support the bank’s Financial Markets business as well as support requirements from other projects with a wider scope such as regulatory books of work. We bring a deep understanding of the market, a scientific approach, and innovative technologies to bear on the challenges of profitable trading in the world's highly competitive environment. This is a rare opportunity to get into an organization working with smart, great technologists globally in financial markets domain. The culture in SABRE is open, intellectual, and fun. Learning opportunities are plentiful: organized classes and seminars; freewheeling discussions; intensive one-on-one mentorships. Career advancement is always waiting for those high-energized talents to pursue. The successful candidate will play a leading role in the implementation of highly visible strategic programmes with the primary responsibilities around building components within the SABRE risk platform. Any developed solution need to be scalable, robust and fault tolerant catering to the existing and future business needs in a timely manner. In addition the successful candidate will be expected to develop innovative solutions with a view to these being released quickly to gain feedback before advising on the full scale roll out. The candidate will be a natural leader and mentor with a driven yet pragmatic, delivery focused mindset and a desire to deliver constant business value through the effective and innovative use of technology with a proven background in delivering high quality business critical software. The candidate will be expected to take ownership, responsibility and accountability for critical deliveries. In addition, the successful candidate will have come from a mature and demonstrable agile background and be comfortable dealing with the resistance that comes with change. An important aspect of the role is second line support in a dynamic and fast-paced front-office environment. The candidate need to be comfortable talking directly with the business to understand the issue and take ownership of the issue to provide a timely and accurate resolution. The candidate will work closely with the other team members and senior stakeholders in order to design and implement critical strategic solutions whilst collaborating and engaging with Business Stakeholders to deliver to exacting requirements. The candidate is expected to work in a dynamic global team with business analysts, project managers, business stakeholders and other technical experts across multiple locations Key Roles & Responsibilities •    Work closely with the Development Manager, Project Manager, and a team of developers to deliver quality features and enhancements to the internal business users •    Demonstrate a systematic and disciplined architectural, system design and programming approach •    Deeply passionate about best-practice software design principles, you will write robust code along with a high-level of test coverage •    Self-starter who can drive/facilitate requirements and development effort •    Design smart and scalable solutions following the bank standards •    Coordinate with other teams in Technology •    Develop, prepare and maintain system documentation, including program descriptions and operational procedures •    Act as the second line of support during production problems. Diversity & Inclusion Standard Chartered is committed to diversity and inclusion. We believe that a work environment which embraces diversity will enable us to get the best out of the broadest spectrum of people to sustain strong business performance and competitive advantage. By building an inclusive culture, each employee can develop a sense of belonging, and have the opportunity to maximize their personal potential. Regulatory & Business Conduct •    Display exemplary conduct and live by the Group’s Values and Code of Conduct. •    Take personal responsibility for embedding the highest standards of ethics, including regulatory and business conduct, across Standard Chartered Bank. This includes understanding and ensuring compliance with, in letter and spirit, all applicable laws, regulations, guidelines and the Group Code of Conduct. •    Lead to achieve the outcomes set out in the Bank’s Conduct Principles •    Effectively and collaboratively identify, escalate, mitigate and resolve risk, conduct and compliance matters. Our Ideal Candidate •	8+ years C#/WPF/.NET development experience building performance critical rich desktop applications •	8+ years hands on development within investment banking IT •	Strong knowledge and experience of working in Agile teams with Agile practices •	Strong understanding of the software development life cycle •	Strong problem-solving and analytical skills •	Strong understanding of technology and the ability to learn new technologies quickly •	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to build and maintain relationships with all stakeholders •	Self-starter that is enthusiastic and proactive •	Be able to work in a fast-paced front-office environment •	Be able to prioritize and work on multiple workstreams simultaneously •	Be able to take ownership of issues and projects, and see them through to delivery •	Possess good judgement and decision making skills with a pragmatic and common-sense approach to solving problems Role Specific Technical Competencies •	C#/WPF/.NET development of rich desktop applications •	Multithreaded, concurrent, and asynchronous programming using TPL and Rx •	MVVM frameworks, preferably Prism •	Dependency Injection containers, preferably Unity •	LINQ/Rx with a functional mindset and coding style •	RESTful APIs and message queues such as ActiveMQ •	DevExpress, Infragistics, or any other popular WPF component library •	Object-Oriented Design and Programming (OOD/OOP) •	NUnit, Moq, TDD, BDD, UI Automation •	CI/CD Pipelines such as Azure DevOps •	Agile/Scrum practices •	Visual Studio and ReSharper About Standard Chartered We're an international bank, nimble enough to act, big enough for impact. For more than 170 years, we've worked to make a positive difference for our clients, communities, and each other. We question the status quo, love a challenge and enjoy finding new opportunities to grow and do better than before. If you're looking for a career with purpose and you want to work for a bank making a difference, we want to hear from you. You can count on us to celebrate your unique talents and we can't wait to see the talents you can bring us. Our purpose, to drive commerce and prosperity through our unique diversity, together with our brand promise, to be here for good are achieved by how we each live our valued behaviours. When you work with us, you'll see how we value difference and advocate inclusion. Together we: • Do the right thing and are assertive, challenge one another, and live with integrity, while putting the client at the heart of what we do • Never settle , continuously striving to improve and innovate, keeping things simple and learning from doing well, and not so well • Are better together , we can be ourselves, be inclusive, see more good in others, and work collectively to build for the long term What we offer In line with our Fair Pay Charter , we offer a competitive salary and benefits to support your mental, physical, financial and social wellbeing. • Core bank funding for retirement savings, medical and life insurance , with flexible and voluntary benefits available in some locations. • Time-off including annual leave, parental/maternity (20 weeks), sabbatical (12 months maximum) and volunteering leave (3 days), along with minimum global standards for annual and public holiday, which is combined to 30 days minimum. • Flexible working options based around home and office locations, with flexible working patterns. • Proactive wellbeing support through Unmind, a market-leading digital wellbeing platform, development courses for resilience and other human skills, global Employee Assistance Programme, sick leave, mental health first-aiders and all sorts of self-help toolkits • A continuous learning culture to support your growth, with opportunities to reskill and upskill and access to physical, virtual and digital learning. • Being part of an inclusive and values driven organisation , one that embraces and celebrates our unique diversity, across our teams, business functions and geographies - everyone feels respected and can realise their full potential. Recruitment Assessments Some of our roles use assessments to help us understand how suitable you are for the role you've applied to. If you are invited to take an assessment, this is great news. It means your application has progressed to an important stage of our recruitment process. Visit our careers website www.sc.com/careers

title: Cost Analyst (Manufacturing)

Translate business needs into analytical problems and collaborate with users to test and refine solutions. Collaboration & Communication Present analytical insights and model outputs to non-technical stakeholders in a clear, structured manner. Participate in problem-solving sessions, innovation discussions, and digital strategy workshops. Contribute new ideas in applied AI, analytics, and maritime digital innovation. Qualifications Minimum Qualifications Bachelors/Masters degree in Maritime Studies, Data Analytics, Data Science, or a related field, graduating in the year 2026 or recently. title: Assistant Professor / Associate Professor in Dynamical Systems and Mathematics of Machine Learning
description: School of Physical and Mathematical Sciences (SPMS) Nanyang Technological University, Singapore Assistant Professor / Associate Professor in Dynamical Systems and Mathematics of Machine Learning Young and research-intensive, Nanyang Technological University (NTU Singapore) is ranked among the world’s top universities. The College of Science seeks a diverse and inclusive workforce and is committed to equality of opportunity. We welcome applications from all and recruit on the basis of merit, regardless of age, race, gender, religion, marital status, family responsibilities, or disability. The School of Physical and Mathematical Sciences at NTU offers multidisciplinary academic programs that provide students with a wide-ranging and up-to-date education. They offer undergraduate training leading to BSc (Hons} and graduate training leading to MSc and PhD degrees. We invite applications for an Assistant Professor / Associate Professor position specialising in Dynamical Systems and Mathematics of Machine Learning . We are looking for world-class candidates with expertise in dynamical systems, numerical analysis, stochastic analysis, and with experience in applying these domains to recurrent neural networks, state-space systems, reservoir computing, learning of dynamic processes, and mathematical learning theory in general. The successful candidate will be expected to teach both undergraduate and postgraduate courses in pure and applied mathematics, conduct high-quality research in the areas of dynamical systems, random dynamical systems, state-space systems, and their applications to machine learning theory and time series forecasting, and promote interdisciplinary collaboration among the disciplines of mathematics, physics, computer science, and engineering that push the development of reliable dynamical machine learning methods for applications. Candidates with strong academic portfolios are encouraged to apply. The successful candidate should actively seek collaborations with research groups beyond his/her research field and leverage on the multidisciplinary ecosystem of NTU. The candidates are expected to: · Establish a world-class research programme. · Enhance the College’s visibility as an international leader in education and research. · Secure research funding from competitive grants and industry partnerships. · Mentor and supervise graduate students and postdoctoral researchers. · Collaborate with interdisciplinary teams within the university and with external partners. · Deliver engaging and effective teaching to both undergraduate and graduate students. · Undertake essential administrative responsibilities within the Schools. Requirements: · PhD in mathematics or a related subject. · Strong research record and relevant publications in reputable peer-reviewed international journals and conferences. · A good track record of supervising research staff, postdocs, students, and interns in relevant fields · Experienced in cross-disciplinary research initiatives and collaboration. · Demonstrated excellence in research, evidenced by a strong publication record in reputable journals · Recognition in the research community, such as invited talks, awards, or memberships in professional societies. · Proven ability to secure research funding. Application Procedure Required application documents: · Cover letter. · Curriculum Vitae, including a full publication list. · Statement of current and future research interest. · Teaching statement. · Names of at least three referees. title: Safety & Integrated Management System Engineer
description: Are you our next creator of change? Do you want to be part of creating solutions for mitigating climate change? At Neste, you will be working for the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel and renewable diesel. Together we will accelerate transformation for a more sustainable future. We are looking for you, Safety & Integrated Management System Engineer to join our Safety Team in the Singapore Refinery You will focus on adopting Neste Operations Excellence Management System (OEMS) and translate it to Singapore refinery Integrated Management System (IMS) with ISO 9001, 14001, 45001, SS651 and best practices. You will provide your expertise to lead our Singapore Refinery IMS, ensuring safety compliance are analysed, updated and sustained. In this job you will collaborate and work closely with the Singapore Safety department. Your key stakeholders will range from engineers, supervisors and front-line personnel in Singapore to the Corporate Safety Team in Finland. As the local administrator of Neste Continuous Improvement Reporting System, you are expected to manage, analyse, trend, gain insights and learnings from Refinery unsafe conditions, near misses, incidents and audit management. In our Safety team, you will report to the Safety Manager and support the safety team in safety-related tasks and achieve our common goals. Together with you and your expertise, we can make an impact today and keep our promises for tomorrow. Reasons to join Real impact right now. We create solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. We live by our values every day; we care, we have courage, we cooperate. Safety and caring. We value safety always and everywhere. We want our work to feel good and do good. At Neste, we nurture a culture where everyone is welcome and feels safe, respected and encouraged to be their unique true selves. Exciting growth. Transformation brings new possibilities, fostering both courage and curiosity. As a global company, collaboration is the key, and we believe in flexible, smart ways of working. We support personal development with versatile learning and career opportunities. More about the job Your role in our team / in creating a sustainable future Audits Lead & coordinate audits, such as ISO certification, recertifications, internal/external audits, regulatory audit (eg. SS651), Corporate Insurance Survey and Neste OEMS (Operations Excellence Management System) Principle / compliance audit and self assessment. Ensure audits action closure, preparation and follow up are sustainable. IMS (Integrated Management System -  Neste OEMS, SS651, ISO 9001, 14001, 45001) Lead, develop and implement Singapore refinery IMS Prepare and facilitate annual Management Review, support the Management Representative, follow up annual Management Review recommendations and track area of improvement closure Be the NMS (Neste Management System) Space-owner and liaise with the cross functional team on QMS  activities (i.e.NMS Space/procedure/Audits) Gatekeeping/Maintaining NMS documentation framework to ensure compliances are met throughout the certification period. Compliance/Legal Register Review quarterly Legal Register related to Health, Safety, Environmental legal requirements & ISO Standards Work with relevant stakeholders to interpret legal requirements Review compliance activities on a monthly basis Owner of Neste Continuous Improvement Reporting System Own Singapore refinery NCR System, processing of NCR reportings, programs owner and be its user administrator Lead & drive Near Miss reporting program such as data trending, analysis, proposals and  recommendations for corrective and preventive actions Incident Learning Own and manage the Incident Learning processes (i.e. reporting, classification, investigation & actions) to ensure compliance with corporate, regulatory & site requirements Advise stakeholders on incident classification and reportable incidents Participate in incident investigations as Safety SME or as team leader Lead cross functional team to trend and propose improvement plans to reduce incident rate and improve safety culture Keys to success in this role / The best combination to succeed in this role Minimum 8 years safety working experience in petrochemical or chemical industry Bachelor’s Degree in chemical / process / process safety engineering; having a Masters Degree in a related field is highly desired Strong understanding and knowledge to lead, support, implement and operationalise IMS to the workforce Able to effectively lead organization in audit preparations, execution, follow up closure and analysis of long term effectiveness, durability and on-going compliance of implemented actions Ability to interpret corporate and regulatory requirements and effectively translate them into practical, organization-specific solutions while ensuring stakeholder alignment Experienced incident investigation leader or facilitator Strong communication, writing and analytical skill Registered WSHO is an added advantage Other strengths we value We value candidates with transdisciplinary thinking, lifelong learning and digital literacy. Let’s get you started We look forward to receiving your CV and application (with a salary request). We will do our best to contact you after the application time has ended. The recruitment process will include interviews and a possible aptitude assessment. The person selected for the position must pass a health examination including a drug test. A security clearance is obtained with your consent. Neste in brief Neste (NESTE, Nasdaq Helsinki) creates solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. The company is the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel, enabling its customers to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions. Neste refines waste, residues and other renewable raw materials to high-quality renewable fuels at its refineries located on three continents. The company’s annual renewable fuels production capacity will be increased to 6.8 million tons in 2027. Neste has high standards for sustainability and the company has consistently been recognized by several leading sustainability indices. In 2024, Neste's revenue stood at EUR 20.6 billion. Read more: neste.com

description:

copy and paste to a new browser: https://chevron.wd5.myworkdayjobs.com/jobs/job/Singapore-DUO-Tower-Singapore/Commercial-Analyst---Analytics---Market-Intelligence_R000068593 Job summary: Chevron is seeking a highly motivated and analytical Commercial Analyst to join our Trading Analytics team. title: Research Analyst
description: Key Responsibilities: Advanced Market Research & Quantitative Analysis Conduct deep quantitative analysis of global dry bulk, tanker, and container shipping markets, integrating physical freight fundamentals and derivatives (FFA) pricing dynamics. Model Development & Data Engineering: Conduct advanced quantitative research on Forward Freight Agreements (FFAs) across dry bulk and/or tanker markets. Design, build, and maintain end-to-end analytical pipelines using Python and R, including data ingestion, cleaning, feature engineering, and validation. Create detailed reports, presentations, and dashboards to communicate findings effectively to stakeholders. 2. Industry Monitoring: Track key industry metrics such as vessel utilization, shipping supply and demand, and pricing trends. Apply version control and follow disciplined coding and documentation standards. Analyse freight market risk using volatility metrics, stress testing, and scenario analysis. Translate complex quantitative outputs into clear commercial insights for trading, chartering, and senior management teams. 4. Collaboration & Communication: Continuously monitor shipping indices, broker reports, and market intelligence sources, critically assessing data quality and bias. Work closely with commercial, trading, and risk teams to support real-time decision-making. Liaise with external stakeholders, such as analysts, consultants, and industry experts, to validate and enhance insights. Able to closely monitor market movements and work overnight when required. 5. Risk Assessment: Identify potential risks and opportunities in trade and shipping operations, including supply chain disruptions and regulatory impacts. Propose mitigation strategies and solutions based on research findings. Requirements: Education Bachelor’s degree or higher in Quantitative Finance, Applied Mathematics, Applied physic, Data Analytics, Applied Analytics, Maritime Studies. Shipping & Market Knowledge Demonstrated experience in shipping research, freight analytics, commodity markets, or freight derivatives (FFA). Strongunderstanding of both physical and paper freight markets, including: 1. Supply/demand mechanics 2. Freight rate formation 3. Forward curves, spreads, and seasonality Prior exposure to dry bulk and/or tanker markets is highly preferred. Experience with derivatives pricing, volatility modelling, or risk management is an advantage. Programming & Technical Skills Strong proficiency in data analysis tools Familiarity with version control tools and structured research or development workflows. Prior experience in financial services, trading, research, or related commercial industries is an advantage. Experience supporting or developing internal tools, reports, or dashboards for business or commercial use. Analytical & Quantitative Skills Strong grounding in statistics, time-series analysis, and forecasting techniques. Ability to independently design analytical frameworks and challenge assumptions. High attention to detail and data integrity. Communication Ability to clearly explain complex quantitative results to non-technical stakeholders. Strong written communication skills for producing structured research reports and presentations.

title: Intermediate Business Analyst (Calypso)
description: Our client is the corporate and investment banking arm of The Group, world's 12th largest bank by total assets. We seek innovative and agile people sharing our mindset to support ambitious and forthcoming technological challenges. We are seeking a talented Business Analyst. As a Business Analyst, you will play a crucial role in bridging the gap between business needs and technical solutions. You will work closely with stakeholders from both your IT Division and aligned sectors to define, document, and translate business requirements into actionable software specifications. You will be a key advocate for business value, ensuring that our software solutions meet the needs of our users and contribute to the overall success of our operations. The Department involves technology solutions and systems used in financial markets for trading, investment, and related activities. This includes electronic trading platforms, risk management systems, market risk, counterparty risk, algorithmic trading, data analytics, and Regulatory measures. The use of advanced technologies like API’s, artificial intelligence and cloud solutions are also becoming increasingly prevalent in capital markets to enhance efficiency and decision-making processes. You will join the Post Trade IT section, responsible for development and maintenance of Capital Market IT applications (in-house and package) used worldwide, covering Front Office (Murex, Apex, Orchestrade…), Back Office (Summit, Calypso…) and serving several sectors or Business lines. The FX IT division works across the full value chain to sustain business activity and enable its acceleration (thru integration of new financial products, optimizing and automating front to back business processes). This specific role aims to work on the Calypso back office package for our FX IT division, in project mode as well as maintenance, and to support the “run the bank” function. It requires a deep understanding of the nature of business operations, timing of activities & data flowing through internal and external systems. The build and support team continuously works with business users, infrastructure engineers and system managers. Responsibilities: • Elicit, analyze, and document business requirements from various stakeholders, ensuring a clear understanding of their needs and objectives for FX and Money Market Business in Calypso. • Work with the Product Owner (PO) if any on this project to prioritize requirements and decompose them into detailed user stories, ensuring a clear roadmap for development. • Translate requirements into comprehensive functional specifications, including detailed descriptions of user interactions, data flows, and system behavior. • Collaborate with development teams to ensure that the software design aligns with the functional specifications and meets the defined requirements. • Provide functional expertise to the development team, ensuring they have a clear understanding of the business processes and user needs. • Research existing solutions, both internal and external, to determine if they can be leveraged to meet the business requirements. • Understand technical constraints and participate in software design discussions, ensuring that the solution is feasible and aligns with the overall system architecture. • Ensure the functional quality of the software, including participation in functional testing and providing functional support during production. • Analyze the impact of functional changes on current production Service Level Agreements (SLAs) and Operational Level Agreements (OLAs). • Contribute to Behavior-Driven Development (BDD) activities by providing user scenarios and supporting functional testing efforts. • Contribute to deployment activities by providing functional pre-requisites and performing post-deployment checks. • Support team members in their functional activities, train new joiners, and help resolve functional issues. • Ensure the seamless integration of the software into the existing IT infrastructure, working closely with the Functional Architect. • Specifications, workload estimates, planning, test scenarios, documentation. Mandatory Skills Description: • Master or Bachelor’s degree in Finance / Computer Science / Information Technology/ Programming & Systems Analysis/ Science (Computer Studies) faculties. • Candidates should have minimum 5 years experience in Business Analysis activities. • Knowledge of Static Data, Workflows, Confirmations, Settlements, Messages / Swift messages, Positions, Accounting, Corporate action, Filters, Reporting Framework, Reconciliation features. • Understanding of financial assets (FX, Money Market, cash products) and trade lifecycle. • Experience with regulatory reporting (e.g. HRF, NORIA, MMSR). • Experience on Back Office systems • Experience in interfacing systems • Familiar with requirements life cycle management, analysis planning and monitoring, requirements' analysis and design definition. • Hands-on experience as liaison function between the onshore IT business lines and offshore technical teams and as primary functional support for development team • Knowledgeable of techniques to elicit and manage requirements, as well as software development models including Agile methodologies. • Exposure to Capital Market FO/BO system (E.g. Calypso, Kondor+, Summit, Murex...) • Knowledge of Static Data, Workflows, Confirmations, Settlements, Messages / Swift messages, Positions, Accounting, Corporate action, Filters, Reporting Framework, Reconciliation features. • Understanding of financial assets (FX, money market, cash) and trade lifecycle. • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to effectively collaborate with diverse teams. • Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills. • Ability to work under pressure. • Appetite to follow technology trend and participate to communities. • Eagerness to learn and adapt to new technologies. • Strong perseverance and diligence towards attaining goals and effective time management • Passion for sharing expertise and grow team members’ skills. • Autonomous, self-motivated and excellent team player Nice-to-Have Skills Description: • Experience ideal on Calypso versions v14 to v18. • Knowledge in UNIX, SQL, Docker/Kubernetes systems.

In this role, you will drive financial excellence and strategic decision-making by providing insightful analysis, robust financial planning, and accurate forecasting. title: Junior Discretionary Trader
description: Squarepoint is a global investment management firm that utilizes a diversified portfolio of systematic and quantitative strategies across financial markets that seeks to achieve high quality, uncorrelated returns for our clients. We have deep expertise in trading, technology and operations and attribute our success to rigorous scientific research. As a technology and data-driven firm, we design and build our own cutting-edge systems, from high performance trading platforms to large scale data analysis and compute farms. With offices around the globe, we emphasize true, global collaboration by aligning our investment, technology and operations teams functionally around the world. Building on our quantitative research platform and process-driven approach, Squarepoint also runs discretionary strategies to take advantage of opportunities which cannot be traded fully systematically. We are looking to hire recent graduates to join our Discretionary Trading team. As a Junior Discretionary Trader, you will have the opportunity to work alongside our senior discretionary traders, who will provide on the desk training and mentorship. Your goal will be to help maintain target portfolio positions by assisting the senior traders with daily portfolio monitoring, maintenance, and hedging. As a collaborative organization, our discretionary traders work closely with quantitative researchers to leverage our world-class research platform to improve both discretionary and systematic strategies. You will leverage and develop a broad range of skills including fundamental and macro analysis, research, portfolio construction, risk management and execution. We currently run discretionary strategies in Equities, Commodities, Rates/FX, and Credit including Volatility trading across all asset classes. If you have an interest in trading and would like to learn how to manage your own risk overtime, we encourage you to apply. Responsibilities: Collaborate with senior traders and quantitative researchers to structure and evaluate new trading opportunities Generate PnL tracking and attribution reports based on trade ideas Monitor and analyze portfolio performance to generate risk reports Hedge books and allocate hedges appropriately Execute large or complex trades which cannot be automated due to complexity or liquidity constraints Qualifications: Recent, or soon to be graduate, with relevant course work, internship, or other applicable experience or knowledge Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative or related field such as Mathematics, Statistics, Econometrics, Engineering, Operations Research, Computer Science, Physics, Economics, Finance Strong communication skills with the ability to collaborate with teammates globally Strong sense of urgency with the ability to work well in a fast-paced environment Programming skills essential, with at least one major programming or scripting language, strong preference for Python.

description: The successful candidate will be a part of the Business Development Team, reporting to the Business Development Manager. The Field Operations Engineer supports the expansion of VISCOY’s market space in Asset Inspection, Monitoring and Maintenance (AIMM) through field operations (proof-of-concepts (POC), product demonstrations and field deployments) and applications development for products in our portfolio. The Field Operations Engineer is the technical expert of our portfolio of Robotics and Digital Technologies. He/she is empowered to experiment and push the technical boundaries of the technologies that we represent in the field. Asset Inspection, Monitoring and Maintenance presents challenges that require the cross deployment of multiple technologies and customisation to the local context to overcome. The Field Operations Engineer leads the field deployment of our portfolio of technologies with our customers with the objective of overcoming these challenges through the development of more effective, cost efficient and safer solutions for the customer’s AIMM regime. The Field Operations Engineer works closely with our Technology Partners and Customers in the scoping, testing and deployment of our products and solutions. He/she will be the bridge between technology and real-world applications in the markets that we serve. To be successful in the role, the Field Operations Engineer is also required to have a good understanding of the limitations of our products. He/she works closely with the Repair and Maintenance Engineer in trouble-shooting, repair and developing the optimal operating parameters for the products in our portfolio. With deep understanding of technology gaps in AIMM, the Applications Development Engineer will be actively involved in scanning the market for relevant new technologies and developments. Technology is not bounded by geography or culture. The successful Field Operations Engineer enjoys the opportunity to travel and work in international teams with Technology Partners and Customers. The role will require the successful candidate to be proactive, possessing initiative, and a strong sense of ownership to take on and see-through projects assigned. He/she will need to collaborate closely both with internal and external stakeholders (technology partners and customers). Responsibilities Support the Business Development Team in: Development of use cases & applications for our Robotics and Digital Technology solutions in Asset Inspection, Monitoring & Maintenance Field Testing and Deployment of our portfolio of Robotics and Digital Technologies Solving AIMM challenges presented by our customers Be an expert of the Robotics and Digital Technologies in our portfolio To establish strong network, build close rapport and work effectively with both internal and external stakeholders. title: Director Automation & Systems Integration (Tuas)
description: At ResMed, we’re on a mission to change lives with every breath — and our global supply chain is rapidly transforming to enable growth, resilience, quality, and value at scale. We’re hiring a Director of Automation & Systems Integration to lead the design, standardisation, and deployment of automation and digital-integration platforms across our manufacturing and distribution network. This role builds the automation backbone of our next-generation supply chain; engineering scalable, reliable, and intelligent systems that increase productivity, improve asset performance, and enable scale. The Opportunity As Director of Automation & Systems Integration leads the global engineering organization responsible for robotics, automation, controls, and systems integration across all supply chain sites. You will define automation design principles and standard platforms, and form strategic engineering partnerships with technology providers and Automation OEMS — going well beyond a “smart customer” to co-create, refine, and scale automation solutions across the network. This role integrates automation with MES, WES/WCS, SCADA, PLC/controls, and digital data layers to enable real-time visibility, predictive insights, and improved decision making. You’ll work closely with Industrial Engineering, Manufacturing Engineering, Global Logistics & Distribution, Planning, Quality, Digital Supply Chain, and Product Development to ensure automation solutions deliver throughput, reliability, safety, and long-term capacity. This role is foundational to our Network 2030+ vision: a globally consistent, modular, automation-ready network capable of scaling with speed, resilience, and confidence. Key Responsibilities Automation Strategy & Network 2030+ Alignment Build and execute a global automation strategy that accelerates automation as the core productivity driver of our next-generation supply chain. Define standardised automation design principles, modular architectures, controls frameworks, and platform strategies deployable across regions. Develop a multi-year roadmap for robotics, AGVs/AMRs, AS/RS, automated test platforms, and material movement automation aligned to network growth and supply chain transformation. Serve as a strategic partner in evaluating automation opportunities and ensuring investments align with business value, operational needs, and supply chain transformation priorities. Systems Integration & the Digital Thread Lead systems integration of automation assets with MES, WES/WCS, SCADA, PLC/Controls IoT, and enterprise data systems Establish integration toolkits, templates, interface libraries, and testing protocols to ensure consistency and reliability. Develop and partner to create standard integration architectures, integration toolkits, and interface libraries that ensure interoperability, scalability, and reliability. Partner with Digital Supply Chain teams to connect automation performance data to real-time dashboards, predictive analytics and digital twin, enabling smarter CapEx and planning decisions . Ensure new and existing automation platforms are integrated securely and consistently, enabling faster deployment and reduced engineering rework. Robotics, Automation Platforms & Deployment Excellence Lead engineering evaluation, technology selection, and deployment of robotics, automation technologies across manufacturing and distribution environments (paperless systems, robotics, LSR automation, AGVs/AMRs, material handling systems, automated test and inspection). Govern engineering specifications, vendor selection, lifecycle cost models, standard controls architecture, FAT/SAT protocols, and commissioning excellence. Establish an Automation Center of Excellence to accelerate standardisation, replicate best practice, and scale solutions globally at pace. Champion design-for-replication to enable rapid, reliable deployment of automation platforms across sites. Asset Performance, Reliability & Automation Health Ensure all automation and integrated systems produce accurate, high resolution performance data to support OEE improvement, predictive maintenance, and capacity optimization. Partner with Industrial Engineering and Asset Performance teams to leverage downtime analytics, bottleneck analysis, TPB aligned reliability engineering practices. Embed automation health monitoring, diagnostics, and predictive analytics to improve uptime, throughput, and line stability. Enable real-time and global automation dashboards, linking physical automation performance directly into planning and continuous improvement. New Site Establishment & Automation-Ready Design Define automation and integration requirements for new supply chain sites, ensuring automation readiness from concept to commissioning. Partner with Facility and Industrial Engineering to embed automation early in layout design, utilities planning, safety architecture, and CVS (Commissioning, Validation, Scale) processes. Deploy modular automation solutions that reduce commissioning times, improve CapEx efficiency,  and enable predictable scalability across the network . Leadership, Governance & Capability Building Build a world-class global team of automation, robotics, controls, and systems integration engineers. Establish engineering governance to ensure global standardization, interoperability, and consistent decision-making across all sites. Create training pathways and capability-building programs to develop future-ready, digitally enabled automation talent. Foster a culture grounded in engineering rigor, innovation, collaboration, and continuous improvement. About You 12–15+ years in automation engineering, robotics, systems integration, or advanced manufacturing engineering, including 5+ years in senior leadership roles. Deep expertise in automation platforms including robotics, AGVs/AMRs, automated test systems, material storage and handling automation, and controls architecture and digital integration (MES, SCADA, PLC, WES/WCS, IoT). Strong manufacturing automation experience is essential; exposure to distribution automation is valuable. Experience with TPM, reliability engineering, or maintenance strategies for automated systems is highly beneficial Demonstrated success delivering global automation deployments in highly regulated, multi-site supply chain environments. Strong understanding of OEE, bottleneck analysis, predictive maintenance, flow modeling, and asset performance improvement. Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Automation, Mechatronics, Systems, Electrical, or Manufacturing Engineering; advanced digital or robotics certifications a plus. Exceptional leadership and communication skills, with the ability to influence senior stakeholders across global supply chain landscapes. Success Metrics Automation Excellence Deliver measurable productivity and flow improvements through scalable automation solutions. Platform Standardization: Drive adoption of standardized automation architectures across the majority of applicable sites within the first 18–24 months. Asset Performance & Reliability Improve uptime and automation reliability through enhanced diagnostics, predictive maintenance, and data-driven monitoring Digital Thread & Integration Velocity Reduce integration time and engineering rework by implementing standard architectures and reusable components. Speed, Scalability & Capital Efficiency Shorten automation deployment timelines at new or expanding sites through modular design and engineering governance. People & Capability Build and sustain a high-performing automation engineering team with strong engagement and development progression. Why Join ResMed This is a rare opportunity to define the automation and integration blueprint of a global, next-generation supply chain. You’ll architect intelligent, connected automation systems that power how we grow, scale, and deliver — enabling extraordinary outcomes for patients worldwide. At ResMed, you’ll join a global team committed to excellence, innovation, and purpose. Together, we’ll build the most integrated, data-driven, and scalable supply chain engineering network in the world.

title: Senior Project Manager
description: Hydroinformatics Institute Pte. Ltd. ("H2i") are looking to strengthen its leadership team with a Senior Project Manager. This is a pivotal role for a professional capable of not only executing complex consultancy and technology development projects in water resources and environmental management but also playing a strategic role in shaping our Consulting business. You will act as a primary interface with our customers, fostering strong partnerships while holding them accountable to project prerequisites and timelines. You will provide leadership to cross-functional teams and contribute directly to the company’s strategic initiatives for growth and market leadership. Key Responsibilities ·      Strategic Leadership & Business Shaping o   Collaborate with the COO to refine delivery methodologies, improve Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), and drive operational excellence within the division. o   Provide mentorship and leadership to cross-functional teams, fostering a culture of accountability and high performance. o   Proactively work with management to develop new business opportunities and lead the preparation of high-quality proposals for new projects. ·      Client Management & Accountability o   Act as the trusted advisor to clients while rigorously managing client-side dependencies. You will hold customers accountable for their deliverables (data, approvals, resources) to ensure project momentum. o   Lead difficult conversations regarding scope creep and change control, ensuring that project objectives remain aligned with commercial agreements. ·      Project Execution & Financial Control o   Execute and manage consultancy and technology development projects related to real-time water resource management (e.g., forecasting systems, water quality modelling) from initiation to post-completion handoff. o   Develop detailed project plans outlining scope, milestones, and resource allocations. Proactively identify risks and implement mitigation measures. o   Financial Management: Manage project budgets (> $1 million SGD) and cash flows, including processing payment claims, assessing financial impacts of changes, and ensuring projects are delivered within budget. o   Reporting: Maintain accurate data in the project management system and report project status to senior management on a bi-weekly and ad-hoc basis. Minimum Qualifications ·   Minimum 8-10 years of experience in managing consultancy and technology development projects, with a proven track record of delivering projects on time and within budget. ·      Master of Science degree in relevant field. ·      Must be legally eligible to work in Singapore. Singapore citizens and PRs preferred. Desirable Qualifications ·      Familiarity with working with government procurement procedures and executing projects in public sector domains. ·      Experience in managing projects with budgets exceeding $1million SGD. ·      Experience or affinity with real-time control applications and operational management systems (e.g. FEWS, MIKE Operations). ·     Experience or affinity in working with water modelling software packages (e.g. WFLOW, Sobek, Mike11, Delft 3D). ·      Experience or affinity in working with management/collaboration tools such as Confluence, Slack, Zoho and Gitlab. ·      PMP certification or equivalent. Skills and Competencies ·      Ability to navigate complex regulatory environments. ·      Excellent communication skills for stakeholder management and reporting progress updates within and outside of own domain. ·      Analytical skills for problem solving and risk mitigation. ·     Strong leadership skills to manage cross functional teams effectively.

title: Sales Executive (Projects & Product Sales)
description: About Us: Linkgas Engineering Enterprise is a leading provider of fire protection services in Singapore, dedicated to ensuring the safety of lives and property through reliable and innovative fire protection solutions. We specialize in the design, installation, and servicing of cutting-edge fire suppression systems and products. Position Overview: The Sales Executive (Projects & Product Sales) is responsible for developing new business opportunities, securing project installations, and managing product sales for fire protection systems. This includes tender participation, technical proposal preparation, and close coordination with the Operations and Design teams. The role focuses on driving growth through project-based revenue and expanding the company’s market reach. Key Responsibilities: 1. Project & Product Sales Identify, pursue, and secure new installation projects and product sales opportunities. Prepare quotations, proposals, and tender submissions for fire suppression and detection systems. Work with technical/design teams to review specifications and ensure proposal compliance. Attend site meetings, pre-tender briefings, and client discussions as required. Follow up on project quotations and negotiate terms and pricing with clients. Coordinate with Operations for project scheduling, execution, and client handover. Track project progress, invoicing, and ensure timely closing of sales orders. 2. Client & Account Development Build strong relationships with consultants, contractors, and direct clients. Promote company products and capabilities to new and existing clients. Support clients with technical clarifications and product recommendations. Ensure smooth transition of completed projects to the Servicing Sales Team for post-installation support. 3. Coordination & Reporting Liaise with the Sales Coordinator for quotation issuance, sales order creation, and documentation in ERP. Maintain project pipeline records and update management on quotation outcomes. Provide accurate monthly forecasts and sales performance updates. Required Qualifications: Diploma or higher in Business, Engineering, or equivalent. At least 2–3 years of project or technical sales experience in the fire protection, M&E, or construction industry. Familiar with tendering procedures, project documentation, and fire protection system components. Proficient in Microsoft Office, AutoCAD (advantageous), and ERP systems. Strong negotiation, presentation, and relationship-building skills. Possess a valid Class 3 driving license preferred.

title: Industrial Solution Proposal Manager
description: The Proposal Manager leads the proposal team to ensure the timely delivery of high-quality technical and commercial proposals that meet customer expectations and support sales targets. This role requires close collaboration with business development teams, technical experts, and other internal and external stakeholders to craft winning proposals for Mega Projects and Digital Transformation (DX) opportunities. Key Responsibilities: Lead and manage the proposal team to ensure consistency, accuracy, and completeness of all proposals, aligning with company standards and customer requirements. Analyze customer RFQs in collaboration with Business Development (BD) teams to define technical and commercial strategy for proposal development. Support proposal development for Mega Projects and DX solutions with a deep understanding of customer needs, competitive positioning, and value proposition. Prepare and submit proposals, including Statements of Work (SOWs), ensuring alignment with internal capabilities and external customer requirements. Respond to RFPs by articulating company’s value proposition, proposing competitive and cost-effective solutions that meet functional, technical, and budgetary requirements. Manage stakeholder communications throughout the proposal lifecycle, including coordination with engineering, project management, legal, procurement, and sales teams. Ensure proper documentation, cost estimation, risk identification, and pricing strategy are embedded in proposals. Oversee order booking preparation and lead the handover process to the engineering execution team upon project award. Requirements: Minimum Degree in Information Technology/Computer Science/Chemical Engineering/Process Engineering Minimum 8 years of working experiences in L3 digital solution such as APC, OTS, PIMS, Asset Management, etc. Related sales engineering or project engineering, preferably in industries like Oil & Gas, Material and life. Relevant experience in ICSS system, such as DCS, FGS, etc. Strong Knowledge and understanding of the major automation vendors, products, services and solutions. Domain knowledge in ISA95, ISA88, OPC UA, MQTT, Modbus, Industry 4.0, MES Best practices and Data Security, Data Availability. Experience in products like PRM, ISAE, Exquantum, Exapilot is the bonus. Strong Technical writing skill with ability to simplify complex technical concepts for non -technical audiences. Detailed-oriented with strong organizational skills

title: Senior Product & Project Engineer
description: Svanehoj Singapore Pte. Ltd . is seeking a Senior Product & Project Engineer to join our growing engineering team. This role is responsible for delivering technical solutions for medium to large-scale engineering projects, with a focus on pump products and fire water pump packages. The successful candidate will work closely with cross-functional teams, suppliers, and customers throughout the full project lifecycle. Main Duties & Responsibilities 1.      Perform technical solutions in medium or large size engineering in pump products and fire water pump packages. 2.      Create engineering drawings and specifications for components, products and mechanical designs working with internal and external customers through the development and approval process. 3.      Maintain quality and accuracy of product master data in ERP system as per latest updates required by sales, purchasing, production and aftermarket. 4.      Provide technical support to customer solution engineering, product improvement within Engineering and to other stakeholders. 5.      Develop, maintain product data books, instruction and operation manuals. 6.      Work with Operations, Marketing, Purchasing, Production, Sales, executive management, and others to develop winning solutions. 7.      Coordinate and consult with other team members to design or detail components. 8.      Proactive technical communication, execution with suppliers, customers, and internal stakeholders during end-to-end project execution. 9.      Identify, mitigate, monitor and report risks in own responsibility area. 10.   Work proactively to promote teamwork at all levels of the company. 11.   Other tasks and special projects as assigned. Pre-requisites Education & Qualification: ·        Bachelor’s degree in mechanical engineering. ·        Experience with mechanical designs involving rotative equipment packages, steel structures, piping and couplings are required. ·        Ability to use AutoCAD, 3D CAD(NX/SolidWorks) required, good understanding of CAD & GD&T standards. ·        Good knowledge on sustaining engineering and ability to use enterprise software. ·        Basic understanding of centrifugal pumps and pumping systems ·        Efficient in using tools like Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint, etc.) and the internet. Experience: ·        5+ years of experience in product development (preferably in pump products and systems in marine applications such as package engineering, hydraulic power packs and diesel engine driven systems)
