Python, Snowflake/Databricks, DBT, Airflow). Push for greater automation and automated testing to improve productivity and reduce operational risks. Become an excellent team player with a collaborative mind-set, you will work directly with investment and technology professionals at different levels. What makes you a successful candidate? Join us if you have a passion for data and want to be part of a team in building and developing GIC’s data foundation and infrastructure. 4 or more years of relevant technology experience preferably in financial risk services or asset management industries, and experience in leading software development teams Good engineering experience & practices in the following:  SQL, and/or relational database – Postgres, Oracle, Snowflake Python/Pandas/DBT/PySpark Java / C# (.NET Core), and/or similar OOP frameworks Unix, Shell scripting (Optional) Docker / Kubernetes / Github workflow, and/or other devops capabilities  (Optional) AWS services such as Glue/Batch, S3, SQS, SNS, EKS (Optional) Federated query tools such as Denodo, Presto/Trino, Starburst Willingness to learn with attention to detail, demonstrating a strong passion in technology and/or finance Fair presentation skills, and communication skills across technical and non-technical audiences Good engagement with software engineers and guide hands-on development Ability to collaborate with cross-departmental business and technical partners to contribute in the delivery of business outcomes Bachelor’s or equivalent degree in Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM) related discipline is advantageous Work at the Point of Impact We need to be forward-looking to attract the right people to help us become the Leading Global Long-term Investor. Join our ambitious, agile, and diverse teams - be empowered to push boundaries and pursue innovative ideas, share your views, and be heard. Be anchored on our PRIME Values: Prudence, Respect, Integrity, Merit and Excellence, which guides us in how we make our day-to-day decisions. We strive to inspire. To make an impact. Flexibility at GIC At GIC, our offices are vibrant hubs for ideation, professional growth, and interpersonal connection.  At the same time, we believe that flexibility allows us to do our best work and be our best selves.  Thus, our teams come into the office four days per week to harness the benefits of in-person collaboration, but have the flexibility to choose which days they work from home and adjust this arrangement as situational needs arise. We pride ourselves on our ability to solve complex technical problems, demonstrate adaptability, work collaboratively as a team, Practice and Company, and share knowledge, problems, and solutions as a team to harness the power of all minds and perspectives. Operating globally, our Team is based in the heart of Singapore near Orchard Central, Central London right next to St Paul’s Cathedral, and in Dubai overlooking to Dubai Marina. Data centres are a key growth area for Cundall, and you will have the opportunity to take ownership of our new and existing projects in the APAC region. You will lead the design and implementation of ICT and Telecoms systems for not only data centres, but across a range of different projects and sectors, with oversight of AV and Smart Building systems where required. As a Senior or Principal Consultant, you will play a key role in requirements capture, technical assessments, and developing strategies and solutions that meet project objectives, industry standards and best practices. You will have the opportunity to contribute to the development of ICT masterplans, site assessments and feasibility studies, design specifications, all whilst collaborating with internal and external multidisciplinary teams. Develop complete design packages including detailed drawings, calculations, technical specifications, employer’s requirements, and tender documentation. Be involved with construction site visits to undertake compliance monitoring roles of ICT works, working alongside other disciplines. Collaborate within the wider Cundall team and external partners, working closely with colleagues of various disciplines to support the delivery of technically excellent and successful multi-disciplinary projects. Support the senior management staff within the team, embracing the collaborative and supportive behaviours Cundall exhibit. This includes line management, team mentoring and chartership mentoring. Contribute to commercial planning and management of projects. The Skills Educated to degree level in Electrical Engineering, ICT or Computer Science (MEng, BEng or BSc), and working towards, or already be, a member of a professional institute. You will be aiming for a career in ICT and telecoms systems design and construction in the built environment. We look for 5-8 years of experience working in an engineering design consultancy or for a contractor. Desired traits for the successful candidate include: Ability to take ownership for tasks and deliverables. Capacity to problem-solve and help guide the team and client towards solutions Organised and confident communicator, with the ability to gather client requirements and lead internal and external meetings Knowledge and experience in designing ICT and telecoms systems: Passive ICT systems, such as structured cabling systems and containment. Active ICT systems, such as Wi-Fi and GSM. Hyperscale and colocation data centre telecoms infrastructure, including outside plant. Satellite communications, antennas, and aerials. MEP facilities for ICT, including power and cooling. Interface between ICT and other building systems, including BMS and Security. Experienced in leading site visits for surveys, witnessing and commissioning, to support design work. Understanding of project resourcing and commercial management would be advantageous. Working knowledge of drawing and model production using Autodesk AutoCAD and Revit. Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications. Excellent communication skills in written and spoken English. WiredScore and/or SmartScore Accredited Professional would be an advantage. Interest or ability to code in languages such as Dynamo and Python would be desirable.

title: Research Fellow
description: About SUTD SUTD is the world’s first Design AI university. With Design AI, artificial intelligence is treated as a partner and a member of the team – not just a tool. This human-AI team concept has been made possible because of SUTD’s unique cohort-based interdisciplinary pedagogy. As a trailblazer in the field of design and technology education and research, SUTD has been pioneering innovative programmes and initiatives since its formation 16 years ago. As part its new mid-term growth strategy called SUTD Leap, it will work closely with industry to co-create solutions in both education and research – for a better world. About The Job We are in search of a highly motivated and accomplished Research Fellow to join our team and significantly contribute to our ongoing research project “AI-Native Token Communication for Physical AI". The successful candidate will collaborate closely with our research team, working on the development of ground-breaking algorithms and methodologies for Physical AI. This role offers an exciting opportunity to engage in cutting-edge research and have a substantial influence on the field. What You'll Do Undertake research in the field of token communication and Physical AI over wireless networks. Develop distributed and scalable inference frameworks and execute experiments to validate the proposed approaches. Co-supervise graduate students and research assistants. Stay up-to-date with the latest advancements in token communication, Physical AI, and related fields, and integrate new findings into research projects. Prepare research papers for publication in prestigious conferences and journals. Deliver presentations on research findings at conferences and other pertinent events. Who We’re Looking For A Doctoral degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Science, or a related discipline, with a specialization on wireless communication and distributed machine learning A strong background in communication theory, signal processing, and optimization. Proficiency in programming languages such as Python and MATLAB. A proven track record of publishing research papers in esteemed conferences or journals in wireless communication and AI. Strong communication skills and the capability to work effectively in a collaborative research environment.

title: Safety Engineer
description: Are you our next creator of change? Do you want to be part of creating solutions for mitigating climate change? At Neste, you will be working for the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel and renewable diesel. Together we will accelerate transformation for a more sustainable future. We are looking for you, Safety Engineer to join our Safety Team in the Singapore Refinery You will focus on supporting and reporting to the Singapore Refinery Operational Safety Manager in the area of operational safety, workplace safety, Emergency and Crisis Management to deliver full compliance with Corporate Safety, local and regulatory safety requirements. In this job you will collaborate and work closely with the safety team, refinery engineers, supervisors and front-line personnel in Singapore and the Corporate Safety Team in Finland. You will provide your operational safety knowledge to our Refinery on matters of operational safety, such as Safety Leadership cultural shift, critical field controls & verifications, Contractor Safety, Workplace Safety & Health Committee, Fire Safety Management, Emergency Response and Crisis management. In our Safety team, you will be required to support the Safety Manager and safety team in safety-related tasks and achieve our common goals. Together with you and your safety knowledge, we can make an impact today and keep our promises for tomorrow. Reasons to join Real impact right now. We create solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. We live by our values every day; we care, we have courage, we cooperate. Safety and caring. We value safety always and everywhere. We want our work to feel good and do good. At Neste, we nurture a culture where everyone is welcome and feels safe, respected and encouraged to be their unique true selves. Exciting growth. Transformation brings new possibilities, fostering both courage and curiosity. As a global company, collaboration is the key, and we believe in flexible, smart ways of working. We support personal development with versatile learning and career opportunities. More about the job Your role in our team / in creating a sustainable future Safety Engineer Drive and execute operational safety, workplace safety, emergency response and crisis management Ensure refinery legal right to operate safely is delivered Support refinery safety manager in strategic and tactical safety planning, implementation and execution of safety programs Operational Safety Support and participate in refinery operational risk assessments, safety critical job and deviations reviews Support corporate and refinery implementation of creating safety together to strengthen refinery safety cultural shift Ensure the executions of critical safety risk controls, monitoring, trending, verifying and communicated for learnings & improvement Workplace Safety Together with refinery key stakeholders, implement contractors and suppliers safety management Support the Turnaround Safety Lead and team in turnaround planning, preparations executions and lesson learnt Support Workplace Safety & Health Committee initiatives and programs Support and execute site fire safety management Incident Management Participate as safety subject matter expert in incident investigation team, facilitate Safety department owned investigations or as directed Prepare, maintain emergency response plan and execute training of company emergency response team to mitigate incidents, emergencies and compliance to National CERT Audits and Standard Support preparation of refinery crisis leader and crisis management team training and dive into the role and responsibility of crisis management team support during crisis Keys to success in this role / The best combination to succeed in this role Minimum 6 years of safety working experience in the oil, gas or petrochemical industry Experienced to support and operationalise workplace safety, fire safety, emergency response and crisis management Good understanding of local WSH regulations and have experience dealing with local regulators such as MHD, MOM, SPF, SCDF, etc. Good communication, writing and analytical skill Recognized Bachelor Degree in Safety or similar, master degree in Safety highly desired Registered  WSHO and FSM, Noise Monitoring Officer added advantage Other strengths we value We value candidates with transdisciplinary thinking, lifelong learning and digital literacy. Let’s get you started We look forward to receiving your CV and application. We will do our best to contact you after the application time has ended. The recruitment process will include interviews and a possible aptitude assessment. The person selected for the position must pass a health examination including a drug test. A security clearance is obtained with your consent. Neste in brief Neste (NESTE, Nasdaq Helsinki) creates solutions for mitigating climate change and accelerating a shift to a circular economy. The company is the world’s leading producer of sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel, enabling its customers to reduce their greenhouse gas emissions. Neste refines waste, residues and other renewable raw materials to high-quality renewable fuels at its refineries located on three continents. The company’s annual renewable fuels production capacity will be increased to 6.8 million tons in 2027. Neste has high standards for sustainability and the company has consistently been recognized by several leading sustainability indices. In 2024, Neste's revenue stood at EUR 20.6 billion. Read more: neste.com

title: Staff Engineer, Gas, Global Facilities
description: Welcome! Are you ready to be at the forefront of ground breaking Engineering within the semiconductor industry?! Micron Technology, Inc., a global leader in memory and storage solutions, is seeking a dedicated and experienced Senior Engineer to join our Gas team within the Global Facilities Engineering department. In this crucial role, you will play a vital part in providing high-quality Engineering services and support for the planning, design, construction, operation, and maintenance of our subject area specific Facilities systems across our Semiconductor Manufacturing sites worldwide for Bulk Gas and Specialty Gas Systems. As a Senior Engineer, Gas for the Global Facilities Engineering department at Micron Technology, Inc., you will be responsible to provide high quality Engineering services and support for the planning, design, construction, operation, and maintenance of your discipline specific Facilities systems in support of the Semiconductor Manufacturing sites across the Micron Global Manufacturing Network – for Bulk Gas systems and Specialty Gas systems. In this role, you will support the Facilities Teams and Manufacturing sites across the network as the Technical Subject Matter Expert through all phases of design, construction, installation, start-up, operations and maintenance with focus and emphasis on Global Standardization and Alignment, Innovation, Reliability and Performance, and Sustainability. Responsibilities: Technical Support, Leadership, and Expertise Act as the Technical Field Domain Expert, offering consultation and support to all Site Facilities and Manufacturing teams. Collaborate with internal partners such as Technology Development, Procurement, Finance, and Legal to champion multi-functional collaboration. Develop, review, and maintain companies global system, equipment, and component Standards, Specifications, and Best Known Methods (BKMs) to ensure excellence in engineering practices. Implement Standardization and Alignment across Micron's Global Network and Sites for Engineering and Design concepts, Construction Project execution, Reliability Programs deployment, and Operations and Maintenance practices. Find opportunities for improvement in system designs to improve performance, efficiency gains, reduce maintenance, and achieve cost savings. Benchmark both within and outside Micron to capture Engineering Best Practices and Well Known Methods for Facilities buildings and systems. Stay up-to-date with industry trends, technological advances, emerging technology roadmaps, design innovations, and sustainability practices. Design and Construction Project Support: Provide valuable support to the Design teams in the Engineering development of new large-scale Facilities Infrastructure Expansion projects. Ensure that designs align with all applicable standards, codes, and regulations. Build and refine system design concepts for deployment. Ensure effective communication with local Facilities, Design, Construction, Procurement, and Manufacturing partners to deliver project objectives seamlessly. Conduct timely and thorough review of design packages, offering detailed comments and feedback to ensure the success of the projects. Assist in providing cost and schedule estimates and justifications for project approvals. Supervise progress and provide engineering expertise during construction as required per project if vital. Participate and lead capacity databases and system operating trends throughout the network, validating utilization and diversity factor assumptions. Review and guide on scope, schedule, and cost estimates for Facilities infrastructure additions or modifications. Global Initiatives Program Support: Take charge of Global Facilities Programs & Initiatives, including establishing and defining program objectives, roadmaps, and high-level implementation plans. Lead Sustainability Programs in alignment with Micron's Corporate Sustainability goals and initiatives. Job Requirements: Bachelor’s Degree in Mechanical, Chemical, Process, or Industrial Systems Engineering required Minimum 7 to 10years experience in Semiconductor and/or Facilities related industry Technical expertise, knowledge, and experience on Facilities and Semiconductor Gas Systems including Bulk Gas Systems, Specialty Gas Systems, Gas Purification, Gas Cabinets and Valve Manifold Boxes, Hazardous and High Purity Gases and Piping, Compressed Air, and Gas Distribution Systems. Ability to prioritize and schedule workload and work on multiple tasks concurrently Self-motivated worker, good organizational skills and service demeanor Excellent communication skills and good team working skills, English Required Familiarity with Semiconductor Manufacturing and Facilities systems Ability to travel internationally 25-40% on average, but up to 50-66% of the time during Critical Project phases or Site Activities

title: Procurement & Logistics Manager
description: Key Responsibilities 1. Supply Chain Planning • Plan material requirements based on project schedules and forecasts. • Manage long-lead items and ensure timely delivery to meet project deadlines. • Coordinate closely with engineering and project teams on technical specifications. 2. Global Procurement & Supplier Management • Source, evaluate, and negotiate with international suppliers for mechanical, electrical, control systems, and automation components. • Conduct RFQ, commercial evaluation, negotiation, and PO issuance. • Monitor supplier delivery performance and ensure quality standards. 3. Logistics & Shipping Management skill / Cross-Border Operations • Handle import/export processes, freight booking, and shipment tracking. • Ensure accurate documentation. • Work with freight forwarders to optimise shipping cost and delivery time. • Coordinate movement of materials between Singapore and Johor plant. • Visit Johor plant for production follow-up. • Ensure compliance with Malaysia customs and logistics procedures. 4. Compliance & Regulatory Knowledge • Understand basic Malaysia regulations related to procurement and logistics (import/export rules, customs processes, MITI where applicable). • Ensure all documentation and shipments comply with Singapore and Malaysia laws. • Maintain audit-ready procurement and logistics records. 5. Inventory & Warehouse Coordination • Work with warehouse teams in Johor to ensure accurate stock levels. 6. Reporting & Internal Collaboration • Provide regular updates on procurement status, logistics risks, and shipment progress. • Collaborate with Engineering, Project Management, Finance, and Production teams. • Maintain accurate data for POs, inventory, and delivery schedules. 7. Continuous Improvement • Enhance supply chain processes, lead time management, and logistics efficiency. • Identify cost-saving opportunities in purchasing and transportation. • Expand supplier base and improve supply reliability. Required Qualifications and Experience • Bachelor’s Degree in Supply Chain, Logistics, Engineering, Business, or related fields. • 5–10 years’ experience in supply chain, procurement, or logistics roles. • Experience in engineering, automation, material handling, or BHS industry preferred. • Strong experience in global sourcing and overseas supplier management. • Strong logistics knowledge: Incoterms, import/export, customs, global shipping. • Strong negotiation and vendor management skills. • Ability to read technical drawings/specifications. • Good communication, analytical, and problem-solving abilities. We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: CIM Presales Solution Architect
description: Mission of the Role As a CIM Presales Solution Architect, you will be the pivotal technical bridge between our innovative CIM/AI product suite and our global clients. Your mission is to architect compelling, technically robust solutions that demonstrate how our technology solves critical manufacturing challenges, ultimately driving commercial success and forging trusted advisor relationships with key stakeholders in the semiconductor and advanced manufacturing industries. Key Responsibilities Partner with the sales team to develop and execute account-specific technical sales strategies for targeted clients. Lead deep-dive discovery workshops to analyze client manufacturing processes, pain points, and technical requirements. Design and present customized solution architectures, demonstrating how our CIM platform integrates AI capabilities to address operational efficiency, yield management, and Industry 4.0 objectives. Develop and deliver high-impact proofs of concept (POCs), technical presentations, and product demonstrations tailored to both technical and executive audiences. Create detailed technical proposals, RFP/RFI responses, and solution documentation that clearly articulate value propositions, implementation approaches, and competitive advantages. Serve as the primary technical advisor throughout the sales cycle, building credibility and managing technical objections. Synthesize market feedback and competitive intelligence to inform product strategy and roadmap priorities. Essential Qualifications Bachelor’s degree or higher in Industrial Engineering, Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field. 5+ years of combined experience in a presales/solutions architecture role AND hands-on implementation or engineering within manufacturing systems (MES, EAP, CIM, SCADA, or related platforms). Strong foundational understanding of semiconductor manufacturing processes (e.g., Fab, Assembly & Test, PCB) or related high-precision industries. Excellent presentation, communication, and storytelling skills, with the ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear business value. Professional fluency in both English and Chinese (written and spoken) is mandatory for client engagements and internal collaboration. Willingness to travel internationally (approximately 70-90%) to support regional sales activities, conferences, and key client meetings. Preferred Qualifications Direct experience with Industry 4.0 standards, digitization projects, or AI/ML applications in a production environment. Practical knowledge of key technologies such as SQL, data modeling, system integration (APIs, SEMI standards), and cloud infrastructure. Proven ability to thrive in a global, cross-cultural team environment.

title: Business Analyst (Contract)
description: Business Analyst (Contract) Location: Singapore Description: The Business Analyst plays a critical role in supporting strategic and operational transformation initiatives across Global Markets Operations (GMO). This role involves analyzing business processes, gathering requirements, and collaborating with stakeholders to design solutions that enhance efficiency and compliance. The successful candidate will work closely with project managers, subject matter experts (SMEs), and technology teams—often in a virtual environment—to deliver high-quality business requirements and process workflows. Strong analytical and communication skills are essential to bridge business needs with technology solutions. Responsibilities: Facilitate workshops and working group discussions with GMO teams across regions, as well as internal and external stakeholders (including Exchanges, Business, and Technology teams). Analyze business and system requirements, identify gaps, and propose solutions aligned with organizational objectives. Develop and maintain Business Requirement Documents (BRDs), functional specifications, and process workflows. Support project managers in managing workstreams, ensuring adherence to enterprise standards and governance frameworks. Collaborate with technology teams to validate requirements and ensure successful implementation. Assist in preparing project dashboards, reports, and documentation for governance routines. Identify opportunities for process improvement and contribute to operational excellence initiatives. Ensure compliance with regulatory, risk, and control requirements throughout the project lifecycle. Promote innovation by introducing new ideas, tools, and methodologies to improve business analysis practices. Requirements: University degree or equivalent in Business, Finance, Technology, or related discipline. Proven experience as a Business Analyst or in a similar role within Financial Services or Operations. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail. Excellent command of written and spoken English; ability to communicate clearly across all organizational levels. Proficiency in MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Visio) and familiarity with business analysis tools (e.g., JIRA, Confluence). Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced, virtual environment. Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple priorities under pressure. Knowledge of AI concepts and experience using AI-powered tools (i.e. Copilot) for reporting or automation. Preferred Skills Experience in Global Markets Operations or Investment Banking. Knowledge of structured business analysis methodologies (e.g., Six Sigma, Lean). Familiarity with Agile project delivery frameworks. Exposure to AI-driven tools and automation concepts for process improvement. Competencies Strategic Thinking: Understand business objectives and translate them into actionable requirements. Business Results: Deliver high-quality analysis that drives successful project outcomes. Collaboration: Work effectively across diverse teams and viewpoints to achieve common goals. Personal Effectiveness: Take ownership of tasks; maintain resilience under pressure. Communication Skills: Facilitate discussions and present complex ideas clearly and concisely.

title: Assistant Engineer, Data Center Facilities Management (1-Net)

As one of the pioneers in broadband development in Singapore, 1-Net currently manages telco-class carrier-neutral Internet Data Centers, along with providing domestic and international connectivity, managed services and media delivery services. 1-Net offers superior stability, secure delivery and service experience through its services. It's uniquely positioned in Southeast Asia, with a comprehensive range of integrated services and a media-centric approach to bring cutting edge solutions to its customers. We are committed to creating an inclusive and diverse workplace where talent thrives. Our hiring decisions are made based on merit and fit-to-role. If you have a disability or special need which requires accommodation to participate in the recruitment process, please inform us when you submit your online application. We will be happy to support as necessary. Thank you for your interest and application to this role. Please note that only short-listed candidates will be contacted. RESPONSIBILITIES This role is to support the engineering team with the daily maintenance and operations of the data center facilities in mechanical and electrical engineering to ensure serviceability of all active and redundancy equipment. This includes performing routine facilities activities such as critical system checks and functional testing. Responsibilities Facilities Operation and Maintenance: Assist the Engineers/ Senior Engineers in the required maintenance and operation activities Respond to alarms/faults, identify trends, and implement corrective actions as needed Respond and investigate Building Management System (BMS)/ Environmental Monitoring System (EMS) alerts, providing feedback on outstanding issues or events, responding to emergency situations, and being on standby duty as required Assist with Data Center projects and any additional and alteration work such as site construction activities and installations, coordination with external contractors and testing in adherence to operational standards within the given timeline Assist with planned and emergency maintenance events and undertake tasks to support the accreditation and certification initiatives Other duties as assigned that are consistent with the responsibilities and objectives of the role Technical and Customer Support: construction, operations requirements to coordinate customer move-in and turn-on, and others Comply to all standard operating procedures (SOP) and relevant facility management matters and procedures QUALIFICATIONS No experience required as training will be provided We welcome the following applicants: Diploma graduates in Electrical or Mechanical engineering or equivalent qualifications Candidates who have enrolled in a Work-Study Diploma program in Data Center Infrastructure & Operation or any related field are welcome to apply under the traineeship program Candidates without the required academic credentials but have practical experience or relevant qualifications in Electrical or Mechanical engineering or equivalent will also be considered 24x7 rotating shift duty Strong communication skills Working location: 1-Net (East) at Chai Chee / 1-Net (North) at Woodlands Please note that your application will be sent to and reviewed by the direct employer - 1-Net Singapore Pte Ltd

title: Technical Manager, EV Charger Development
description: Power the future of mobility in Singapore Empowering Journeys. Building a Greener Tomorrow. Do you want your engineering work to leave a visible, lasting impact on Singapore? Are you excited by the challenge of building critical infrastructure that supports the nation’s transition to electric vehicles? EV-Electric (EVe) Charging Pte Ltd is the organisation behind Singapore’s public EV charging network. Wholly owned and fully backed by the Land Transport Authority (LTA) , we are leading the nationwide deployment of EV chargers and the electrical infrastructure upgrades that make cleaner transportation possible. As demand for EVs accelerates, we’re looking for passionate, detail-driven engineers to join us and help shape the future of sustainable mobility. About the Role As Technical Manager, Charger Development , you will play a key role in the design, deployment, and commissioning of EV chargers across public car parks island-wide. You will work closely with internal teams, contractors, and government agencies to ensure projects are delivered safely, efficiently, and to the highest standards. This is a hands-on role for someone who enjoys seeing projects move from concept to reality—and knowing their work benefits commuters. What You’ll Be Doing EV Charger Development & Deployment Support end-to-end design development and deployment of EV chargers in public car parks Monitor site progress to ensure works are delivered on schedule and to specification Support electrical and civil-related site matters during infrastructure upgrades Tender & Contract Management Develop tender specifications, technical requirements, and supporting documentation Ensure deployment works comply with contract requirements, relevant codes of practice, and regulatory standards Testing, Commissioning & Compliance Manage and support testing and commissioning of EV Charging Points (EVCPs) Coordinate with relevant agencies on approvals and inspections Ensure all testing, commissioning, and handover documentation is properly completed and maintained Team & Project Support Support operational and administrative functions within the team Lead or support ad-hoc projects and continuous improvement initiatives What you are expected to demonstrate in this Role Business Awareness & Execution – Understand industry trends, align work with organizational goals, and suggest practical improvements Innovation & Problem Solving – Propose creative ideas, test new approaches, and refine solutions Evidence-Based Decision Making – Analyze data, identify patterns, and make sound technical recommendations Collaboration & Teamwork – Work respectfully with colleagues, share knowledge, and contribute to team success Stakeholder Engagement – Communicate effectively with contractors, agencies, and partners to build trust and alignment Adaptability & Growth Mindset – Embrace change, learn from challenges, and continuously develop your skills What We’re Looking For Qualifications & Experience Tertiary qualification in an Engineering discipline At least 2 years of relevant working experience in engineering, project management, or site supervision Candidates with less experience will also be considered if they demonstrate strong organisational skills, teamwork, and leadership potential Skills & Attributes Experience in project coordination, site supervision , and administrative support Strong interpersonal and communication skills to work with diverse stakeholders High attention to detail and strong documentation discipline Proficient in Microsoft Office applications A proactive, responsible, and solutions-oriented mindset Why Join EVe? Work on nation-critical infrastructure with visible impact across Singapore Be part of the EV transformation journey in a fast-growing sector Enjoy the stability and purpose of an LTA-backed organisation Collaborate with passionate professionals committed to sustainability and innovation If you’re ready to apply your engineering skills to something that truly matters, we’d love to hear from you. Join us and help power Singapore’s electric future. ⚡🚗

Ensure automation solutions are scalable, maintainable, auditable, and aligned with governance standards. Support user testing, data validation, rollout, and post-implementation support. title: Backend Engineer (Platform)
description: Sunollo is building a premium residential energy platform that combines high-quality hardware, reliable installation operations, and software to make clean energy simple for homes in Singapore. The initial focus of this role is to build the foundation of our billing platform, followed by broader platform and internal systems. This role is suitable for a senior backend engineer who enjoys high ownership, clear scope, and building production systems that are reliable and maintainable. title: Senior Data Scientist
description: Who We Are Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact. To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive. We Are BCG X We’re a diverse team of more than 3,000 tech experts united by a drive to make a difference. Working across industries and disciplines, we combine our experience and expertise to tackle the biggest challenges faced by society today. We go beyond what was once thought possible, creating new and innovative solutions to the world’s most complex problems. Leveraging BCG’s global network and partnerships with leading organizations, BCG X provides a stable ecosystem for talent to build game-changing businesses, products, and services from the ground up, all while growing their career. Together, we strive to create solutions that will positively impact the lives of millions. What You'll Do Our BCG X teams own the full analytics value-chain end to end: framing new business challenges, designing innovative algorithms, implementing, and deploying scalable solutions, and enabling colleagues and clients to fully embrace AI. Our product offerings span from fully custom-builds to industry specific leading edge AI software solutions. As a Data Scientist, you'll be part of our rapidly growing team. You'll have the chance to apply data science methods and analytics to real-world business situations across a variety of industries to drive significant business impact. You'll have the chance to partner with clients in a variety of BCG regions and industries, and on key topics like climate change, enabling them to design, build, and deploy new and innovative solutions. Additional responsibilities will include developing and delivering thought leadership in scientific communities and papers as well as leading conferences on behalf of BCG X. Successful candidates are intellectually curious builders who are biased toward action, scrappy, and communicative. We are looking for talented individuals with a passion for data science, statistics, operations research and transforming organizations into AI led innovative companies. Successful candidates possess the following: Comfortable in a client-facing role with the ambition to lead teams Likes to distill complex results or processes into simple, clear visualizations Explain sophisticated data science concepts in an understandable manner Love building things and are comfortable working with modern development tools and writing code collaboratively (bonus points if you have a software development or DevOps experience) Significant experience applying advanced analytics to a variety of business situations and a proven ability to synthesize complex data Deep understanding of modern machine learning techniques and their mathematical underpinnings, and can translate this into business implications for our clients Have strong project management skills What You'll Bring Master’s Degree or PhD with significant relevant experience providing advanced analytics solutions. The degree should be in computer science, applied mathematics, statistics, machine learning, or a related data centric field. Demonstrated deep technical and data science expertise, acute strategic and analytical skills, ability to lead and persuade, drive and energy, and desire to work in a project-based environment on strategic issues. Comfortable in a client-facing role with the ambition to lead teams Likes to distill complex results or processes into simple, clear visualizations Explain sophisticated data science concepts in an understandable manner Strong record of professional accomplishment and leadership. Fluency in Python Additional info Ability to travel based on client and business needs.

title: Mechanical Design Engineer
description: Job Purpose We are seeking a highly skilled and self-motivated Mechanical Design Engineer capable of independently handling mechanical design tasks. The candidate will be responsible for designing, analyzing, and optimizing mechanical systems and components for various projects, ensuring compliance with industry standards, safety regulations, and client requirements. The engineer will also participate project tender and site to understand customer enquiry and expectation, develop concept design and support procurement team, communicate with customer, vendor and supplier to improve proposal process and quality. Key Responsibilities 1. HVAC System Design : Establish cleanroom design according to client desire and production need, prepare cleanroom architectural layout dwg by using AutoCAD, Revit, or other design software. Develop mechanical HVAC system design following cleanroom design, prepare HVAC P&ID. Calculate mechanical load requirements and size components such as HVAC heatload calculation, fan ESP sizing, pump head calculation, duct/pipe sizing, HVAC equipment model selection. Establish HVAC control philosophy write-up, cause and effect analysis, develop alarm list and interlock mechanism. 2. Mechanical System Design : Establish process utility design following customer’s tools matrix summary, such as CDA, Vacuum, PCW, Steam, Exhaust, Drain, Specialty Gas, RODI, WWTP and etc Develop process utility P&ID and piping/ducting layout. Develop plant room space management study and services routing from plant room to production floor. Calculate mechanical load requirements and size components such utility pump/fan sizing, pipe/duct sizing, utility flow calculation. Establish process utility system control philosophy write-up, cause and effect analysis, develop alarm list and interlock mechanism. 3. Project Tendering : Review tender requirements, participate site visit, communicate with client to ensure the proposals meet client and project specifications. Develop concept dwg and equipment schedule to enhance vendor and supplier knowledge on tender specification and specific challenges. Participate tender costing exercise, support procurement team on proposal preparation. Support procurement team to evaluate vendor/supplier proposal qualification, materials/equipment selection, ensure proposed solution sound practicable and reasonable, meeting quality and regulatory compliance, adhere tender scope of work, delivery schedule, deviations and partial compliance element. Collaborate with vendors to improve competitive pricing for project tenders. 4. Project Management : Coordinate with clients, contractors, and project stakeholders to clarify requirements and resolve design-related issues. Conduct system design presentation to explain proposed design, equipment selection, comparison of different design approach, convince client’s doubt and persuade business decision. 5. Documentation : Prepare technical documents, includes MEP dwg, equipment schedule, RFQ/RFP, sub-contract work package specifications. Maintain accurate and up-to-date project files and records. 6. Collaboration : Work closely with the electrical, control, civil, and architectural teams to ensure seamless integration of HVAC and mechanical systems into overall project designs and execution. Review and evaluate supplier/vendor proposal, assist QS and project team on equipment selection and seek supplier/vendor clarification on design specification compliance and performance assurance. Qualifications and Skills 1. Educational Background : Bachelor’s degree in mechanical, manufacturing, process engineering, or Industrial related engineering field. 2. Experience : Minimum of [4] years of experience in mechanical design engineering, with a proven track record of handling projects independently. 3. Technical Skills : Proficient in mechanical design software such as AutoCAD, Revit or similar tools and Microsoft Office. Decent knowledge of hvac and mechanical codes and standards (e.g. ASHRAE, ISO-14644, SMAGNA) Computer skills - ACAD, MS Project, BIM Experience with project bidding, develop BOQ, material sourcing, compile project quotations. 4. Soft Skills : Strong problem-solving and analytical skills. Self-motivated and capable of working independently with minimal supervision. Effective communication: capable of drafting clear transmittals, datasheets and technical queries; comfortable liaising with vendors and site teams to clarify requirements. 5. Additional Skills (Option) : Electrical LV wiring design experience, such as cable sizing and develop SLD for LCP of mechanical system. Understand general control and instrumentation logic diagram for mechanical system. Understand BIM and able to perform simple BIM modelling. Mobility as when needed.

title: Research Fellow (Engineering - Mechanical/ Clean/Renewable Energy/ Building related)
description: Established in 2010, the Energy Research Institute @ NTU (ERI@N) is a pan-university research institute that focuses on systems-level research for tropical megacities. It performs translational research that covers the energy value chain from generation to innovative end-use solutions, motivated by industrialisation and deployment. ERI@N has multiple Interdisciplinary Research Programmes which focus on translational Research, Development & Deployment which focus on specific area of the energy value chain, and a number of Living labs and Testbeds which facilitate large scale technology deployment enabling validation and demonstration of real-world applications. For more details, please view https://www.ntu.edu.sg/erian You will be part of a dynamic research team working on topics relevant to the Sustainable Built Environment (SBE). The SBE team focuses on building energy modelling and simulation, decarbonization strategies for the built environment and R&D of green building technologies such as HVAC systems, thermal energy storage, data-driven building control systems and other novel solutions for the urban built environment sector. This position is to recruit a member of the research team to work on a project on sustainable indoor farming. Key Responsibilities : You will need to actively support SBE projects at ERI@N in terms of project R&D, technology evaluation, project coordination, and stakeholder interfacing. The role will include the following: Perform as subject matter expert for ongoing projects e.g. in energy modelling and/or embodied carbon assessment; coordination with project partners, industry and other external stakeholders. Provide inputs for conceptualization of research proposals related to sustainable urban solutions, green buildings, low-carbon district level solutions and agritech applications. Lead in preparation and submission of new research proposals. Conduct research, analysis and assessment on embodied carbon for buildings, urban farming and other applications. Lead technical analysis and research on green building technologies, decarbonization strategies for new and existing buildings and smart building systems incorporating AI/ML/energy analytics. Engage stakeholders at management and working levels internally as well as with external funding agencies, collaborators and industry partners, including delivering presentations and guiding discussions or workshops as necessary. Lead in authoring of technical publications for completed or ongoing research projects. You will be an integral part of the SBE team and will need to participate in project discussions especially related to project implementation details. Job Requirements PhD degree in engineering (e.g. Mechanical, Clean/Renewable Energy or Building related) or sustainable buildings design. Experience in sustainable design/analysis for the built environment, Green Mark or LEED projects, M&E construction and development (min 2yrs preferred). Proficient in MS Office applications, BIM software and energy modelling software. Should be a very hands-on person, willing to take on non-routine tasks. Possess good communication (written and verbal) skills. Able to manage multiple stakeholders and work under tight timelines. Positive attitude, ability to work independently as well as in a team. Familiarity with green building technology, HVAC system study and optimization, sustainable building design, building energy modelling, CFD modelling, BIM and embodied carbon/lifecycle assessment for built environment applications is preferred. Knowledge in EN 15978 or similar standards for carbon assessment or lifecycle analysis. Prior experience in sustainability standards and green building accreditations would be advantageous. title: Operations Executive
description: Company Background Raffles Tankers, incorporated in 2021, was founded with the sole purpose of operating its owned vessels, which had previously been placed on long-term contracts with leading Oil Majors and global trading houses. Over time, the company expanded its footprint through strategic acquisitions, bareboat charters, and time charter arrangements—allowing it to capitalize on freight market volatility and grow a diversified fleet. Driven by an entrepreneurial spirit, Raffles Tankers continues to pursue organic fleet expansion at a safe, sustainable pace. Today, the company operates and controls a range of tonnage, including LR2s, MRs, and Chemical Tankers. With a strong focus on operational excellence, Raffles Tankers is committed to delivering safe, responsible, and reliable marine transportation services while fostering a dynamic and collaborative work environment. Job Description Raffles Tankers is seeking a Full-Time Operator/Operations Executive for an on-site role based in Singapore. The Operations Executive will manage day-to-day vessel operations, ensuring accurate documentation of vessel activities, earnings, and expenses. The role includes coordination with Vessels, Charterers, Brokers to facilitate smooth vessel operations, as well as identifying opportunities to optimize efficiency. Responsibilities also involve operational planning, proactive problem-solving, and effective resource management to support organizational goals. Responsibilities ⁠Manage day-to-day vessel operations and ensure all activities comply with charter party terms and operational requirements. ⁠Plan and coordinate voyages by working closely with Masters, agents, the chartering team, ship managers, owners, bunker suppliers, and other relevant stakeholders. ⁠Prepare and issue Letters of Indemnity (LOIs) in line with charter party conditions for both internal and external charters. ⁠Liaise regularly with charterers to ensure smooth operations, minimise delays, and maximise vessel performance and commercial returns. ⁠Appoint port agents, review port disbursement accounts, and verify third-party invoices to ensure accuracy and cost-efficient operations. ⁠Maintain strong service standards by ensuring operational accuracy and proactive communication with agents, brokers, and the wider network. Requirements ⁠Strong problem-solving skills, proactive mindset, and ability to work independently under pressure. ⁠Good interpersonal skills. ⁠Able to fit in a Multicultural & Multinational Environment. ⁠Proficient in Microsoft Office; vessel optimisation, or performance tools is an advantage. ⁠Demonstrates continuous learning and willingness to collaborate across the team. ⁠Open to occasional overseas travel if required. Fresh Graduates with a willingness to learn in a fast-paced shipping environment may apply.

title: Manager/ Senior Manager, Process and Quality Control (Fab Operations) 3 Years Renewable Contract
description:
Job Description: Leading a team in responsible for the strategic design, implementation, and continuous improvement of all quality and process control systems within Fab Operations. This role is critical for ensuring world-class product yield, reducing defectivity, and maintaining strict compliance with customer specifications, internal quality standards, and relevant industry certifications. Key Responsibilities: Develop and drive a strategic roadmap for yield improvement and defect reduction across critical
     process modules. Lead the design and maintenance of advanced, real-time Statistical Process Control (SPC) , Fault
     Detection and Classification (FDC) , and Advanced Process Control (APC) systems to stabilize and optimize all fabrication steps. Cross-contamination management to achieve balance between research and development flexibilities in
     integration run sheet flow path vs process tools health and process repeatability. Small volume prototype protocol and product and process control plan alignment and set-up with requestor, process margin runs till demonstration of product SPC repeatability and line defect and electrical yield. Process Window Definition: Collaborate with Process Integration and R&D teams to define, qualify, and stabilize robust manufacturing process windows for all new product introductions (NPI), ensuring Design for Manufacturability (DFM) principles are met.      performance goals, developing career paths, and fostering a culture of ownership and data-driven decision-making. Collaborate closely with all stakeholders to balance the trade-off between quality stability, process efficiency, and tool uptime. Preferable 10+ years of experience in the semiconductor industry, with a minimum of 5 years in a leadership/management role focusing on Process Control, Quality Assurance, or Yield Engineering within a Fab environment. Deep technical knowledge of semiconductor fabrication processes in different modules. Expert-level knowledge of Statistical Process Control (SPC) , Design of Experiments (DOE) , Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) , and Lean/Six Sigma methodologies (Black Belt certification highly preferred). Strong leadership, presentation, and interpersonal skills, with the proven ability to influence and align technical and executive stakeholders. ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified. description:
As
a Quantity Surveyor, you will play a key role in the commercial delivery of our
interior fit-out projects. Reporting to senior commercial management and
working closely with Project Managers and design teams, you will manage the
cost, procurement, and commercial aspects of projects from tender through to
final account. This
role suits someone comfortable working in a fast-paced Design & Build

cost control. Key responsibilities : 1.       Cost Estimation and
Budgeting ·      
Prepare accurate cost estimations for interior fit-out

Develop project budgets and ensure adherence to budgetary

requirements ·

Measurement ·

Documentation ·

Experience as a Quantity Surveyor in interior fit-out or
construction projects ·      
Practical experience managing procurement, variations, and
subcontractor accounts ·      
Comfortable working in short-duration, fast-track project
environments ·      
Strong attention to detail, commercial awareness, and
communication skills ·      
Proficient in Excel. ·      
Able to speak and write in English and Mandarin

title: Actuarial (Financial Services) Senior Consultant, Risk Consulting
description: At EY, you’ll have the chance to build a career as unique as you are, with the global scale, support, inclusive culture and technology to become the best version of you. And we’re counting on your unique voice and perspective to help EY become even better, too. Join us and build an exceptional experience for yourself, and a better working world for all. The Opportunity As part of our life actuarial team, you’ll focus on high-impact opportunities to apply your knowledge and experience that will shape our services. You’ll build valuable relationships with clients and develop strong capabilities, through both formal training and working with senior mentors and talented colleagues. You will be at the forefront of issues that are shaping the insurance industry in Asia and gain a wide exposure to a diverse range of actuarial projects. Your Key Responsibilities As a Senior Consultant in the Actuarial team, you will be assisting and leading the delivery of a wide range of client engagements, leading or working as part of a project team, relating to IFRS 17, Appointed Actuary work, actuarial valuation, pricing and product development, actuarial modelling, Merger & Acquisitions (M&A) and due diligence. You will also be performing evaluation in areas related to financial risk, product distribution and capital issues. You will also work with advanced financial modeling approaches and systems, e.g. designing valuation and Asset Liability Management (ALM) frameworks. Skills and Attributes for Success Good understanding of economic or market issues and the ability to interpret their impact on clients Possess strong interpersonal and teaming skills Has strong analytical and creative skills Leverage technology to continually learn, improve service delivery and maintain our leading-edge best practices To Qualify for the role, you must have Min 3 years of experience in the life actuarial team or an actuarial consulting firm Newly or Nearly qualified with either the Institute and Faculty of Actuaries (UK), the Society of Actuaries (US) or the Institute of Actuaries of Australia Good knowledge of Prophet software Good written and verbal communication skills Ideally, you’ll also have Depth of experience in at least one of the following areas: Financial modeling Financial and regulatory reporting Product and distribution strategy ERM / Solvency II M&A due diligence What we look for Highly motivated individuals with excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to prioritize shifting workloads in a rapidly changing industry. An effective communicator, you’ll be a confident team player that collaborates with people from various teams while looking to develop your career in a dynamic organization. What working at EY offers We offer a competitive compensation package where you’ll be rewarded based on your performance and recognized for the value you bring to our business. We also offer you: Support, coaching and feedback from some of the most engaging colleagues around Opportunities to develop new skills and progress your career The freedom and flexibility to handle your role in a way that’s right for you If you can confidently demonstrate that you meet the criteria above, please contact us as soon as possible.

title: Cybersecurity System Engineer
description: ORGANISATION CONTEXT The Airspace Mobility Solutions (AMS) business, part of the Global Business Unit Land and Air Systems, is providing leading edge Air Traffic Management (ATM), Unmanned Traffic Management (UTM), Security and Digital Aviation solutions for the Commercial and Defence sectors. The AMS business manages 11% of the world’s airspace and with revenues of over 500 million Euros worldwide, is the global market leader in airspace management solutions.  In Singapore, the business focus is the delivery and support of two key ATM solutions including TopSKY-ATC(HE) and LORADS-III. Support security patch management Lead where assigned the integration and verification of security controls Lead where assigned the application and operation of procedural and technical security controls Contribute to cybersecurity test campaigns Contribute to Security risk assessments Deliver fundamental-level cyber security training in an accessible, easy-to-understand manner Promote knowledge sharing ensuring that work is captured sufficiently for re-use across projects Follow Thales processes and practises and contribute to their improvement KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS & EXPERIENCE Qualifications Degree in Engineering, Computer Science or related discipline majoring in Cybersecurity Postgraduate studies in Cybersecurity (Desirable) Essential Experience At least 5 years of relevant cybersecurity experience Strong knowledge in Cybersecurity solutions (e.g. IAM, PAM, SIEM, EDR, Keys, Firewall) Strong knowledge in Cybersecurity engineering activities Knowledge of Systems Engineering Lifecycle and relevant standards eg. ISO/IEC15288 Demonstrated ability to achieve effective outcomes in a multidiscipline, multi-culture environment A positive attitude combined with excellent interpersonal and motivational skills. Sound judgement and independent decision-making capability where necessary Excellent written and oral communication skills in English Desirable: Industry qualifications, such as CISSP Experience in system hardening and hardening review based on CIS Benchmarks or DISA STIG frameworks. Knowledge of ISO 27001 foundational requirements. Experience in country security regulations (e.g. CCOP and IM(ICT&SS) for Singapore) Experience in ATM Domain and related standards eg. ICAO, Eurocontrol , or safety critical systems. Experience in System Engineering tools eg. DOORs, Polarian, Jira Experience in vulnerability management and threat modeling Experience in working in a Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe) GENERAL / SPECIAL REQUIREMENTS Assist in ensuring a harmonious work environment in all departments that you are working with by upholding Thales' key values. title: Technical Solutions & Product Engineer
description: 🔐 ABOUT SECUREVISION Securevision is a Singapore-based security systems integrator specialising in intelligent,IP-based security solutions. We design, deploy, and support integrated systems including CCTV, video analytics, intercoms, access control, alarm systems, and cloud-based platforms for residential, commercial, and institutional clients. As we continue to expand our portfolio of brands and solutions, we are looking for a Technical Solutions & Product Engineer to strengthen our technical backbone and product capability. 📌 ROLE OVERVIEW The Technical Solutions & Product Engineer sits at the intersection of product engineering, technical support, and solution enablement. You will be responsible for understanding our systems at a deep level, validating new products and software, supporting internal teams, and translating technical complexity into deployable, supportable solutions. You will work closely with management, sales, installation, and service teams, and play a key role in shaping Securevision’s technical standards and knowledge base. 📝 KEY RESPONSIBILITIES 1. PRODUCT & SOLUTION ENGINEERING Test, evaluate, and validate new products, firmware, and software platforms before adoption Understand security systems at an architecture and system level , including: CCTV, NVRs, and VMS platforms Video intercom systems Access control systems Alarm systems IP networking and cloud components Assess system stability,integration feasibility, deployment constraints, and limitations Provide structured technical feedback and recommendations to management. 2. TECHNICAL SUPPORT & TROUBLESHOOTING Provide 2nd / 3rd level technical support to installation and service teams Troubleshoot system, software, and network-related issues Support customers primarily via remote troubleshooting, with on-site support for complex cases when required Assist in root cause analysis and preventive improvements. 3. KNOWLEDGE BASE & TRAINING ENABLEMENT Build and maintain Securevision’s internal technical knowledge base Summarise vendor manuals into practical operational guides and reference materials Develop internal training decks and technical learning materials for technicians Use AI tools where appropriate to accelerate documentation and knowledge creation. 4. SALES & PROJECT ENABLEMENT Prepare and support system demos Participate in early-stage project discussions and technical solutioning sessions Support technical aspects of proposals and presentations as required Attend project meetings where technical input is needed to shape viable solutions 🎓 REQUIREMENTS Diploma or Degree in Engineering (Electrical / Electronics / Mechatronics), IT, Networking, or a related discipline 2–6 years of experience in security systems, IP-based systems, or enterprise IT environments Strong understanding of IP networking concepts (IP addressing, ports, basic network troubleshooting) Comfortable with software configuration, firmware updates, and system validation Able to work independently and think at a systems level 🧠 PREFERRED EXPERIENCE 1. Hands-on experience with one or more of the following: CCTV and VMS platforms Video intercom systems (e.g. Akuvox or similar) Access control systems (e.g. ZKTeco or similar) Alarm systems (e.g. Risco or similar) 2. Experience conducting product demos or technical training 3. Familiarity with cloud-based or mobile security applications 4. Comfortable using AI tools to assist with learning, documentation, and summarisation 🤩 PREFERRED ATTRIBUTES Strong systems thinker with the ability to connect multiple technologies Clear communicator who can explain complex technical concepts simply Confident in offering alternative or contradictory technical viewpoints when appropriate Detail-oriented, structured, and methodical Curious, fast learner, and adaptable to new technologies. Able to converse professionally in English and Chinese (spoken) to our local customers & vendors Able to read and understand technical documentation in English for audit purposes. Valid Class 3/3A driver’s license for site visits and travel preferred. 💪  WHAT YOU’LL GAIN Exposure to a wide range of modern security technologies and platforms Opportunity to shape product adoption and technical standards A role with strong growth potential into senior technical or solutions leadership Direct involvement in real-world, system-level problem solving 📌 ADDITIONAL INFORMATION This is a Singapore-based role Foreign candidates are welcome, provided they meet role requirements and language expectations Occasional on-site support may be required for complex technical cases 💰 SALARY & BENEFITS Salary Range commensurate with experience Performance bonus, Annual leave & medical benefits Friendly and supportive team environment 🕒 WORK SCHEDULE Location: Singapore, Near Paya Lebar MRT Monday to Friday 8:30 AM to 6:00 PM Occasional overtime may be required.

title: Full Stack Software Developer - A26016
description: Activate Interactive Pte Ltd (“Activate”) is a leading technology consultancy headquartered in Singapore with a presence in Malaysia and Indonesia. Our clients are empowered with quality, cost-effective, and impactful end-to-end application development, like mobile and web applications, and cloud technology that remove technology roadblocks and increase their business efficiency. We believe in positively impacting the lives of people around us and the environment we live in through the use of technology. Hence, we are committed to providing a conducive environment for all employees to realise their full potential, who in turn have the opportunity to continuously drive innovation. Co-Development Business Unit is looking for Full Stack Software Developer This is a fixed term contract role. The engagement is 1 year. Internal Code: A26016 You will be part of the team responsible for the full stack design, development, and maintenance of software testing toolkits that support the Air Traffic Management Support (ATMS) systems. These toolkits are essential for validating the functionality and performance of the ATM support systems, which support essential Air Traffic Control (ATC) operations such as flight plan processing and NOTAM dissemination. You will be involved in the end-to-end software development lifecycle, including frontend and backend development, system integration, testing, deployment of the software testing toolkits. You will work closely with system engineers, testers, and operational users to ensure the toolkits meet operational, technical, and regulatory requirements. What will you do? Design and develop full stack testing toolkits and simulators for ATM support systems, covering both frontend interfaces and backend logic. Build intuitive and responsive user interfaces for test configuration, execution, and reporting using modern web technologies. Develop backend services and APIs to support automated testing and data simulation. Integrate with third-party services, APIs, and gateways as required Implement test automation frameworks and CI/CD pipelines to streamline testing and deployment processes. Monitor application performance, troubleshoot issues, and implement optimisation strategies Collaborate with system engineers and testers to define test requirements and ensure comprehensive coverage of operational scenarios. Ensure the toolkits are scalable, maintainable, and aligned with software engineering best practices. Maintain technical documentation including system design and review, user guides, test plans, and system architecture. Ensure compliance with aviation standards (e.g., ICAO, Eurocae) and cybersecurity requirements in the development of testing tools. Provide technical support during system integration, testing, and commissioning phases. Stay current with emerging technologies and recommend improvements to development processes. What are we looking for? Minimum 2 years of experiences in full stack software development, preferably in mission-critical or safety-critical systems. Proficient in frontend technologies such as HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript/TypeScript, and frameworks like React, Angular, or Vue.js. Strong backend development skills using Python, Java, Node.js, or similar languages. Experienced with RESTful APIs, microservices architecture, and database systems (SQL/NoSQL). Experienced with system simulation, data modelling, and test data generation. Familiar with modern software practices including version control, automated testing, CI/CD pipelines, code reviews, and agile methodologies Experience with Cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure) and containerization (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) is a plus. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with attention to detail. Good communication and documentation skills. Organised and self-motivated, able to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines. May be required to travel for system integration or stakeholder engagements. What do we offer? Fun working environment Employee Wellness Program To work in Singapore Government Agencies projects We provide structured development framework and growth opportunities. (We are a “SHRI 2025 Gold winner” in “Learning & Development; Coaching & Mentoring”) Why you'll love working with us? If you are looking for opportunities to collaborate with leading industry experts and be surrounded by highly motivated and talented peers, we welcome you to join us. We provide all employees with equal opportunities to grow and develop with us. We believe your success is our success. Does it sound like something you are interested in exploring further? Please be in touch with our team for an initial chat at chi@activate.sg Activate Interactive Singapore is an equal opportunity employer. Employment decisions will be based on merit, qualifications and abilities. Activate Interactive Pte Ltd does not discriminate in employment opportunities or practices on the basis of race, colour, religion, gender, sexuality, national origin, age, disability, marital status or any other characteristics protected by law. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data are longstanding top priorities for Activate Interactive Pte Ltd. Your personal data will be processed for the purposes of managing Activate Interactive Pte Ltd’s recruitment related activities, which include setting up and conducting interviews and tests for applicants, evaluating and assessing the results, and as is otherwise needed in the recruitment and hiring processes. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.activate.sg/privacy-policy) to know more about how we collect, use, and transfer the personal data of our candidates.
