The team also facilitates enablement (education and training), to drive best practice in the Field. What You Will Do: Focus on customer engagements, early in the sales cycle of our most strategic customers (driving both new lands and expansions), with the goal of identifying and communicating the business value of our solutions. Support account planning Complete deep-dive discoveries with prospects and customers Create & deliver business value deliverables Craft winning value propositions. Act as a subject matter expert for deal structuring, including complex pricing strategy, and value propositions, leveraging Confluent value selling methodologies. Support sales teams on the structuring of strategic, complex, and competitive deals, applying deal best practices Engage customer Executives and Partners to communicate Confluent’s vision and value proposition, around Data in Motion. This can include presenting at events and running roundtable discussions. Maintain an evolving subject matter expertise in Confluents portfolio and on-prem, cloud and hybrid deal constructs. What You Will Bring: Bachelor’s degree. 7+ years experience in Business Value Consulting, Value Engineering, Strategic Consulting, Deal Strategy, Sales, Finance or customer negotiation type roles. Financial analysis and modeling of ROI and TCO concepts to justify business cases. Expertise in driving organizations toward greater effectiveness and efficiency. Thorough understanding of all industry-standard and non-standard software selling models including SaaS, perpetual, on-demand, consumption, etc. An interest in Data; Enterprise Architecture, Infrastructure - and an aptitude to learn about Data in Motion and Confluent’s portfolio. Awareness of the Data industry and SaaS / Cloud industry in general. Proven track record of improving deal velocity alongside Risk Management. Passion for using data to drive decision making. Superior contract negotiation skills.  Strong abstract thinker able to apply complex concepts across a variety of situations. Proven project management experience; successfully leading and completing projects. Experience working with remote Sales and business partner teams. Strong oral and written communication skills with the ability to synthesize and effectively communicate information across teams, roles, and levels. Experience working with companies that have public cloud, hybrid cloud and on-premises deployment options. description:
Job Title - Robotics Integration Engineer Location - Singapore Start Date – ASAP Contract - 1 year extendable Job Description Develop and support interfaces for robotic arms and gantry robots on

Integrate these robots with MES and other control systems such that robotic actions (pick, place, assemble, etc.) are triggered by software commands and reported to systems in real time. Work with PLC/SCADA systems that control conveyors, sorters, turn tables, shuttles, and other auxiliary devices;
implement middleware or IoT gateways to bridge PLC signals with the factory IT layer. For example, ensure that when a robot places a unit on a conveyor, the PLC detects it and the MES is immediately updated with the unit’s location. Must Have Skills Experience integrating industrial robotic arms into automated production lines, including using vendor APIs or standards (OPC UA, ROS-I) to command and monitor robots. Solid
understanding of PLC/SCADA systems and how to interface with them –able to interpret and utilize signals from PLCs to synchronize robot actions with conveyors or other equipment. Strong programming skills (C# or Java preferred) for writing middleware that coordinates between robots, PLCs, and higher-level IT systems. Good to Have Skills: Hands-on knowledge of PLC programming or configuration, which aids in troubleshooting and fine-tuning the interface between IT and control systems. Familiarity with robot motion programming and error handling, enabling quicker diagnosis of robot faults or misalignments. Understanding safety standards and interlocks for robotic cells (to ensure the software integration upholds all required
safety stops and guard checks).

Steer management toward data-driven decision-making by revamping dashboards, automating reporting, and generating analytical insights that influence strategic outcomes. Partner with internal stakeholders to deliver data solutions that provide insights, projections, measurable metrics, and performance tracking — including usage trends for semiconductor materials and related product portfolios. Qualifications Master’s degree / PhD in Engineering, Materials Science, Economics, Data Science, Business Analytics, or a related field. 10+ years’ experience in market analytics, strategic insights, or market research —within semiconductors or electronics high-tech sectors with global and APAC remit . Strong expertise in addressable market modeling, materials segmentation, forecasting, data visualization, and reporting across products and materials markets. Experience with semiconductor industry data sources (equipment, materials, fabrication, packaging) and research vendors covering Global & APAC markets . Proven ability to manage large, complex datasets and ensure high standards of data integrity . Proficiency with Tableau and dashboard development; experience with other BI/analytics platforms is a plus. Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills with the ability to influence stakeholders and translate complex data into actionable business insights.

Cross-team technical leadership Influence technical direction across multiple teams through design reviews, RFCs, and hands-on collaboration. Mentor senior engineers and help shape engineering best practices without formal people management responsibilities. Platform and infrastructure evolution Contribute to the evolution of core infrastructure and shared platform capabilities to improve developer productivity and system reliability. Evaluate new technologies pragmatically and guide their adoption where they deliver clear value. Who you are We are looking for senior engineers with deep technical expertise and a strong track record of impact as an individual contributor. Deep experience designing and delivering large-scale, distributed systems in production. Strong computer science fundamentals and solid understanding of object-oriented design. title: Control Room Operator | FPSO | 2 Years
description: Job Description: Responsible for the efficient and effective control of the process plant operation and production-related equipment for the assigned unit, in compliance with MODEC procedures, national and international regulations and standards, thus ensuring the unit's availability, safe operation and the production and revenue target achievement, contributing to the business continuity and its growth. Overall Job Purpose Responsible for the efficient and effective control of the process plant operation and production-related equipment for the assigned unit, in compliance with MODEC procedures, national and international regulations and standards, thus ensuring the unit's availability, safe operation and the production and revenue target achievement, contributing to the business continuity and its growth. Core Responsibilities Control and carry out process plant operation and production-related equipment via FCS and other control panels, under the direction of Production Supervisor and with the support from Operators' team, including operational routines, monitoring, controlling and operational optimization of the process plant, sampling and chemical analyses, start/stop and isolations of equipment, commissioning/decommissioning and operational alignments/maneuvers, in order to ensure that the production systems of the platform are operated according to the project and its operating philosophy, thus optimizing production performance and chemical consumption. Contribute to the unit's annual budget planning (expenses and investments), by participating of the corporate planning, providing information about resources, replacements, services and materials needs and other expenses used in its activities, liaising with Supervisors and Technicians to ensure accurate information. Contribute to an effective response to emergencies on board by being part of the Emergency Response Team, participating in all trainings, simulations and acting properly in a real situation, in accordance with MODEC's procedures, guidelines, as well as national and international regulations and best practices. Support the offshore and onshore leaderships in the interface and contact with the client, authorities and internal stakeholders., by providing faithful information regarding operation's and control room routine and indicators, when requested by Production Supervisor. Contribute to the MODEC’s Permit to Work management systems' implementation, by acting as Permit user of PTW for the task performed, in accordance with MODEC's procedures. Contribute to the continuous improvement of fleet performance by participating on, when requested, RCAs, investigations of NOIs/NODs, HAZID/HAZOPs, following up or acting upon CAPAs, sharing lessons learned and promoting a culture of continuous seek for performance and integrity enhancement opportunities. Act proactively to safeguarding MODEC from noncompliance to legal/regulatory requirements by following MODEC's procedures, management systems, client guidelines, internal and external regulations, national and international standards, participating in audits and inspections in the unit, developing and ensuring the implementation of action plans when required. Guarantee the proper use of Technical Support departments, by ensuring that technical queries are properly identified, recorded in the system, and always directed to Onshore departments for review/study, promoting a culture adherent to the company's systems. Contribute to enhance MODEC's procedures, by constantly evaluating the department activities and seeking improvement opportunities, sharing lessons learned with Technical Support departments and Operations leadership, participating in procedures' review, following MODEC's Philosophy, manufacturer recommendation, and personnel experience. Ensure the proper communication regarding the department activities developed onboard, by assisting in the preparation of the OIM Daily Report, as well as periodic and on demand reports for the client, authorities, or leadership. Communicate to the Production Supt all production or safety systems' emergency, by providing accurate and relevant information immediately, request technical advice as necessary and prepare, follow up and close out any necessary vendor assistance on board for the Production department, in liaison with relevant leaderships. Contribute to the operational performance by inhibiting and overriding controller, when required, in accordance with MODEC's procedures, and properly registering all occurrences in the production and safety / emergency systems. Assume the responsibilities of the Production Supervisor whenever designated by the OIM or Production Superintendent, complying with all training, procedures and responsibilities relevant to the function. Contribute with the quality of Production works performed by core crew or vendors, by providing them with adequate and suitable information, as directed by the Production Supervisor. Contribute to the proper response to emergencies and shutdowns, understanding and applying PSD, ESD and C&E (cause & effects diagram) diagrams. HSSE Requirements and Code of Conduct HSSE: Act in compliance with the corporate HSSE Management System Policy, the MODEC Life Saving Rules, and the policies, guides, process standards and work instructions available in the Integrated Management System (IMS). Act to promote the preservation of the environment, the health and occupational safety (personnel and their team), the safety of processes, and the preservation of assets in their scale of responsibility. Ethics and Compliance: In compliance with the Code of Business Conduct and Ethics, as well as with MODEC Group Ethics and Compliance Policies and Local Procedures, their respective forms, online and live mandatory trainings is committed to comply with all applicable laws, regulations and obligations and act in accordance with the highest ethical standards and with MODEC values. Emergency Plan: As per indication on the Training Matrix, Emergency Management Guide and Offshore Emergency Management, available in the Integrated Management System (IMS). Financial and Operational Dimensions / Impact and Decision-making Authority Complies with Authorization Matrix Procedure, available in the Integrated Management System (IMS). Work Relationships As per indication on the Organizational Chart, provided by the company. Job Challenges and Problem Solving Monitor all production indicators while guiding the Operators' team in the field maneuvers, due to high level of simultaneous attention that both activities require. Work in shifts due to continuous operation and constant personnel presence required, being necessary to invert the shifts of work during the offshore period. Qualifications & Experience Minimum 5 years' experience is expected; Previous experience in Offshore/Onshore Oil & Gas area is expected. Previous experience as Production Operator in F(P)SO units is required. Previous experience in Production Control Rooms is required. Previous experience and knowledge of hydrocarbon processing and utility systems are expected. Key Behaviors: Complies with the Job Competence Model, provided by the T&D&P team. Languages: Fluent English is mandatory. Software: Microsoft Office suite (PowerPoint, Excel, Word) Working Conditions Complies as a highest importance and priority with safety, security, protection of environment and occupational health conditions for the job position.

title: Marine Operations Executive
description: About the job CEVA Logistics, a world leader in third-party logistics, provides global supply chain solutions to connect people, products and providers all around the world. Headquartered in Marseille, France, CEVA Logistics offers a broad range of end-to-end, customized solutions in contract logistics and air, ocean, ground and finished vehicle transport in 170 countries worldwide thanks to its approximately 110,000 employees at approximately 1,500 facilities. With a pro forma 2023 revenue of US$20.2 billion, CEVA Logistics is part of the CMA CGM Group, a global player in sea, land, air and logistics solutions. YOUR ROLE The Marine Logistics Executive serves as the primary point of contact for marine customers regarding ship spares logistics in Singapore. The role is responsible for the end-to-end coordination of import, clearance, warehousing, and delivery of marine shipments, while ensuring operational excellence and customer satisfaction. WHAT ARE YOU GOING TO DO? Act as the main contact for marine customers regarding ship spares logistics. Provide standby support for urgent shipment enquiries from vessel captains, service engineers, bunker surveyors, purchasers, etc. Coordinate with internal stakeholders and customers Receive pre-alerts (air, ocean, courier, self-delivery) from global customers and vendors. Arrange and coordinate shipment delivery via trucking, courier, and other modes. Prepare Pick List from Logiswift WMS to send to warehouse Monitor vessel ETA daily via Portnet and update customer inventory accordingly. Open job files in Cargowise, ensure timely cost accrual, billing, and upload of Proof of Delivery (POD) to eDocs. Prepare and submit permit declarations for shipments via TNETS. Generate shipping documents including Delivery Notes, Ship Supply Notes, and Delivery Advice for drivers. Send pre-loading advice to vessel and purchasers 24hours before. Liaise with local product (Air/Ocean) team to ensure timely customs clearance and delivery to the Marine warehouse. Assign drivers based on urgency and route, serving as the traffic coordinator for collection and delivery. Ensure all customer emails are responded to within 24 hours. Obtain rates from product teams and provide quotations for cross-border trucking and courier services. Prepare and send inventory reports to customers and follow up on any enquiries or discrepancies. WHAT ARE WE LOOKING FOR? Diploma or equivalent qualification in Logistics, Supply Chain, or related field. Minimum 3 years of experience in freight forwarding or marine logistics preferred. Strong knowledge of import/export procedures, shipping documentation, and customs clearance. Proficient in logistics systems (e.g., Cargowise) and Microsoft Office applications. Able to work independently, handle multiple priorities, and remain calm under pressure. Willingness to be on standby for urgent marine logistics support. WHAT DO WE HAVE TO OFFER? With a genuine culture of reward and recognition, we want our employees to grow, develop and be part of our journey. We offer a benefits package that includes competitive annual leave entitlement, pension, health, and life assurance. There’s no doubt that you will be compensated for your hard work and commitment so if you’d like to work for one of the top Logistics providers in the world then please do get in touch to find your next role. ABOUT TOMORROW We value your professional and personal growth. That’s why we share plenty of career opportunities for you to thrive within CEVA. Join CEVA for a challenging career.

title: Finance Shared Services Centre (FSSC) Manager
description: Reolink Group is establishing a regional Finance Shared Services Centre (FSSC) headquartered in Malaysia, to centralize and standardize finance operations across its global entities (Asia, Europe, and the Americas). Head of FSSC will be responsible for the design, implementation, and ongoing leadership of the Shared Services Centre — ensuring best-in-class service delivery, process governance, automation, and scalability to support Reolink’s fast-growing global e-commerce operations. This is a strategic and hands-on leadership role requiring deep expertise in finance transformation, process standardization, automation (RPA/ERP), and cross-border compliance. The role partners closely with the Singapore HQ and global business leaders to ensure strong governance, control, and data-driven decision support across the enterprise. Job Responsibilities 1. Leadership & Strategy Establish the FSSC organization structure, governance framework, and operating model aligned with Group CFO directives. Lead end-to-end transitions from legacy finance processes in multiple countries to centralized SSC operations. Define and implement Service Level Agreements (SLAs) and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) for each tower (R2R, P2P, O2C, Tax & Treasury, FP&A, MDM, Internal Control). Partner with senior business leaders across e-commerce, logistics, and digital sales to ensure finance processes are tightly integrated with commercial operations. 2. Operational Management Oversee all day-to-day operations of the FSSC, ensuring accuracy, timeliness, and compliance. Manage financial transaction processing for AP/AR, GL, consolidation, and reporting. Establish a governance and escalation matrix for multi-region issue resolution. Continuously review and improve workflow efficiency through process re-engineering and automation. Monitor SLA/KPI performance dashboards and implement corrective measures when needed. 3. Systems, Automation & Analytics Oversee ERP configuration and integration (SAP / Oracle / NetSuite or equivalent). Implement RPA-Robotic Process Automation, OCR-Optical Character Regonition, and workflow automation for high-volume, repetitive finance activities. Drive data integrity and analytics through master data management and BI dashboards (e.g., Power BI, Tableau). Collaborate with IT to ensure system access control, data privacy, and cybersecurity compliance. 4. Compliance, Control & Governance Ensure compliance with IFRS, local statutory requirements, tax regulations, and transfer pricing standards. Implement strong internal control frameworks and coordinate with internal/external audit teams. Establish segregation of duties (SOD), approval hierarchies, and audit trails across all systems. Oversee documentation of SOPs and process flow manuals for all functions. 5. People Leadership & Capability Development Build and manage a high-performing SSC team across Malaysia, with clear career paths and functional specialization. Mentor tower leads and ensure succession planning for key roles. Foster a culture of collaboration, accountability, and continuous learning. Implement structured onboarding and technical training for all SSC employees Job Responsibilities Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or related field. Professional Qualification (mandatory): CPA, ACCA, CA, or equivalent. MBA or Master’s in Finance preferred. Minimum 15 years of progressive finance experience, with at least 8 years in a Shared Services or Regional Finance leadership role. Proven record of setting up or transforming finance SSCs for multinational or e-commerce companies. Deep expertise in R2R, P2P, O2C, and FP&A processes, with practical exposure to consolidation and intercompany operations. Experience managing cross-border finance operations across Asia / Europe / North America. Strong background in ERP systems (SAP, Oracle, NetSuite) and automation tools (RPA, OCR, BI). Familiar with digital commerce financial models — including online payment reconciliation, multi-currency settlements, platform integrations (Amazon, Shopify, Shopee, etc.), and chargeback control. Strategic thinker with operational discipline and execution focus. Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills across diverse cultures. Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset, with ability to drive change and influence outcomes. Proven ability to lead transformation projects and manage large multi-functional teams.

title: Cloud Solution Architect - Copilot AI Business Solutions
description: Overview The Customer Success Architect (CSA) for AI Business Solutions is responsible for enabling Microsoft’s enterprise customers to achieve business transformation through AI-powered solutions. Operating within the Customer Success Unit (CSU), the CSA plays a strategic role in leading technical engagements across AI Workforce and AI Business Process portfolios. This includes designing and implementing secure, scalable, and resilient architectures using Microsoft technologies such as Microsoft 365 Copilot, Copilot Studio, and Power Platform. The CSA guides customers through the entire Copilot journey, from envisioning to deployment and adoption, applying value-based delivery frameworks and Success by Design methodologies. In this capacity, the CSA serves as a trusted advisor and advocate for customers and partners, translating feedback and insights into actionable solutions that align with industry best practices. Beyond technical delivery, the CSA acts as a business strategist and customer advocate, translating feedback and insights into actionable solution designs that align with industry trends and customer goals. The role demands strong business value literacy, the ability to articulate ROI and transformation impact, and experience in change management and customer success planning. The CSA identifies opportunities to drive consumption and usage growth, ensures operational excellence, and collaborates across engineering, field, and partner teams to scale delivery and support unified customer success. This position is recognized for technical excellence, innovation, business strategy, and customer obsession. Responsibilities Operates according to required operational excellence and proven practice standards throughout all sales stages/activities/tools of record (e.g., pipeline updates, time tracking). Collaborates across Microsoft and customer/partner teams through on-strategy delivery to achieve customer/partner objectives and increase customer/partner satisfaction. Proactively manages business and technical risks, adapts methodology and applies governance principles to identify, communicate, and minimize business and technical risks. Executes work in compliance with industry and Microsoft guidelines and procedures. Continuously prioritizes among competing demands in their work and identifies where impact occurs with customers, ensuring alignment with business priorities and goals. Delivers solutions in line with company methodologies (e.g., product offerings such as value-based deliverables [VBDs], advice, training, technical validation), ensuring proven practices and patterns are followed to prepare customers for operational readiness and achievement of their business goals and targets. Leveraging knowledge of change management proven practices and/or involving the change management team, proactively helps the customer deploy for long-term organizational adoption to increase customer satisfaction and drive consumption/usage. Guides customers/partners towards a well-architected (e.g., secure, resilient, artificial intelligence [AI]-enabled), and cost- and performance-optimized solution to increase retention and expansion opportunities. Provides feedback to Unified Delivery Team on VBDs to refine and further develop content. Proactively identifies new opportunities for Consumption, Usage & Unified expansion (especially Enhanced Solutions) at scale based on business value to customer/partner and clear understanding of the Microsoft value proposition for supported platforms to empower cloud success, foster and strengthen security and resiliency, and drive AI innovation. Actively listens to and respectfully challenges customers/partners, building trust to then advocate for alternative architectures/solutions/approaches that shape and/or enhance customer requirements. Identifies, and evaluates industry trends (e.g., customer industry verticals, information technology [IT] industry), gathers customer/partner insights (e.g., feedback around technical preferences, environments, business needs, competitive landscape), and maps existing architecture and digital transformation solutions to customer/partner business outcomes. Proactively captures opportunities in appropriate systems, processes, and tools, working collaboratively across the organization to execute on opportunities aligned with Microsoft's Customer Engagement Model. Proactively identifies, escalates, and mitigates blockers using appropriate tools and processes to accelerate solution deployment, value realization and usage/consumption. Applies business, technical, industry, and/or enterprise knowledge to architecture or support projects to meet business and information technology (IT) requirements and resolve identified constraints. Customer Centricity Proactively acts as the voice of the customer/partner, sharing ideas, feedback, insights, success stories and strategic/technical input with Engineering teams, Product Offerings teams and internal communities leveraging relevant insights from feedback tools and systems. Identifies patterns of feedback across customers/partners/territories/industries and leverages them to develop strategic and actionable insights. Presents business cases to program managers to influence product roadmaps, decision making, bug fix prioritization, and own and drive initiatives as appropriate. Identifies and/or translates customer/partner problems into industry solutions aligned with Microsoft product and platform strategy, and explains why and/or how they meet customer/partner outcomes and return on investment (ROI) goals (e.g., via proof of concept, minimally viable product [MVP], rapid prototype) relative to competitive offerings. Proactively helps the customer/partner adopt and use Microsoft product/platform strategy-aligned (cross-solution area) solutions. Builds relationships with technical decision makers (TDMs), and helps build the bridge between TDMs and business decision makers (BDMs). Independently interacts as needed with customers/partners' Chief Information Officer (CIO), Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) and other C-level (CXO) roles to bridge understanding of security, compliance, operational and risk requirements across BDMs, TDMs, CIO and CISO teams, appropriately engaging additional subject matter experts when deeper expertise is required. Drives self and guides other team members to focus on customer/partner experience through efficient delivery and ensuring a seamless and connected customer experience. Defines customer/partner conditions of success by leveraging an understanding of customer goals. Anticipates and addresses customer/partner dissatisfaction and unmet needs, and executes strategies to improve customer experience, value realization, and acceleration of transformation. Identifies and prioritizes several opportunities that align with revenue goals, and orchestrates growth of solution utilization pipeline with partners. Provides assistance with partner and sales teams to bring clarity to specific opportunities through proof of concept and technical pre-sales support. Technical Leadership Leverages market insights and demand signals to assist leadership in identifying relevant areas in which to drive up-skilling and/or accreditations based on demand. Role models technical readiness, both depth-aligned to solution area priorities and breadth-aligned to Customer Success Unit (CSU)/corporate initiatives (e.g., security, resilience, AI), and influences team to drive their own technical readiness. Acts as a mentor, leading readiness and upskilling activities in the team/organization by educating colleagues on technical and non-technical concepts and sharing proven practices. Proactively identifies gaps through delivery, communicates those gaps to relevant team members and internal/external stakeholders, and connects gaps and patterns across business and technology areas that drive changes and improvements to products, IP (both existing and new), technologies, and/or processes/practices that enable solutions to scale across customers. Contributes to the development and modification of Microsoft's structured frameworks and methodologies. Provides thought leadership and innovation to customers/partners and internal communities at the local level. Contributes to leading virtual teams (v-teams) around technologies and customer/partner challenges, and shares ideas, insights, and strategic, technical input with technical teams, internal communities across the field, and the larger virtual team across Microsoft using knowledge of Microsoft architectures and their context in the competitive landscape. Demonstrates strong industry knowledge and increases recognition for Microsoft solutions by contributing to and sometimes leading presentations and engagements with external and internal audiences (e.g., Tech Connect, Build, Ignite) Contributes to intellectual property (IP) and identifies patterns where no IP exists (e.g., CoPilot and AI scenarios) to help build scalable and repeatable models. Participates in external technical and non-technical community events (e.g., conferences, seminars, technical meetups, webcasts, blogs, hackathons) that elevate the Microsoft brand and shares learnings across internal teams Qualifications Driving Customer Outcomes Understanding and mapping customer requirements to the company's offerings effectively. Identifying opportunities, translating customer needs into tailored solutions, ensuring that products or services precisely meet customer expectations, resulting in high levels of satisfaction and success. The ability to identify business needs and determine solutions to business problems; solutions may include a software-systems development component, process improvement, organizational change or strategic planning and policy development. This includes expressing empathy and compassion when dealing with the needs and problems of others, being approachable, taking time to address the concerns of coworkers, and treating others with respect and dignity The ability to interact confidently with senior leaders of the organization in order to present/defend/clarify concerns or issues regarding an existing project, program, or solution. This includes the ability to confidently address difficult questions, handle push back from a high-level audience, and maintain an executive demeanor while engaging in difficult or sometimes high-pressure situations. Required/minimum qualifications Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, Business, Liberal Arts, or related field AND 4+ years experience in cloud/infrastructure technologies, information technology (IT) consulting/support, systems administration, network operations, software development/support, technology solutions, practice development, architecture, and/or consulting OR equivalent experience. Technical Certification in Cloud (e.g., Azure, AI,  Power Platform, M365 and security certifications). Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. title: Network Infra Specialist
description: Position Objective The Network Infrastructure Specialist manages and supports the IT infrastructure systems and operations within the school campus. This role includes monitoring infrastructure performance and resolving technical issues. The modernisation of the infrastructure is currently in progress, and additional efforts will be needed to promote the adoption of cloud solutions to enhance the efficiency of school operations. You will adopt a critical and analytical approach to solving technical problems. Your responsibilities will include taking preventive measures, monitoring infrastructure operations, receiving incidents reported by the Service Desk, and resolving these incidents. The goal is to minimise disruptions to IT services, ensuring smooth daily operations for the school. Responsibilities Key Responsibilities but not limited to: •	Manages the entire infrastructure, including networks, servers, WLC, security systems (Firewall, IPS), and special network services (DHCP, DNS, VLAN, Routing Protocols) •	Provide Level 2 and higher support for IT infrastructure-related incidents. Address reported incidents and manage escalations until a workaround is identified and normal service is restored. Update the incident status in the IT Service Desk System. title: Project Engineer
description: About Us: Kim Hua Construction is a leading construction firm specializing in high-quality hotel, commercial, and residential projects throughout Singapore. Known for our commitment to excellence, innovation, and sustainability, we deliver exceptional projects that meet the unique needs of our clients. We are seeking a Project Engineer to support our construction teams and contribute to the successful execution of key developments. Job Summary: As a Project Engineer at Kim Hua Construction , you will be responsible for supporting the Project Manager in coordinating all engineering aspects of construction projects. You will ensure that the project is executed in accordance with plans, specifications, budget, and timeline. The role involves close collaboration with subcontractors, suppliers, and internal teams to maintain quality control, safety standards, and overall project efficiency. Key Responsibilities: Project Coordination & Support: Assist the Project Manager in the daily management of construction projects, ensuring alignment with the approved schedule, budget, and design. Coordinate and monitor the work of subcontractors and suppliers to ensure compliance with project specifications and timelines. Review and ensure the technical accuracy of project drawings, plans, and construction documents. Quality Assurance & Control: Conduct site inspections to monitor quality, adherence to technical specifications, and compliance with safety regulations. Resolve technical issues that arise during the construction phase and provide solutions to prevent project delays. Implement and enforce quality control procedures and safety standards on-site to maintain the highest standards of work. Procurement & Material Management: Assist in the procurement of materials, tools, and equipment required for the project, ensuring timely delivery and cost-efficiency. Track inventory levels and assist in managing material stocks to avoid delays or shortages during the construction process. Documentation & Reporting: Prepare and maintain accurate project records, including progress reports, site diaries, and other necessary documentation. Track and report any changes to the scope of work and help ensure that changes are properly documented and communicated to all relevant parties. Assist in preparing and submitting reports to the Project Manager, clients, and stakeholders, highlighting progress, challenges, and upcoming milestones. Problem-Solving & Technical Support: Provide technical support and guidance to construction teams, addressing any engineering or design-related issues on-site. Collaborate with architects, engineers, and designers to implement changes or adjustments to plans as required during construction. Coordinate the resolution of any technical challenges with relevant parties to minimize project disruptions. Health, Safety, and Environmental Compliance: Ensure that all site activities comply with health, safety, and environmental regulations. Support the implementation of safety protocols and conduct regular safety audits on-site. Promote a safety-first culture and provide training or support to staff as needed to ensure safe working conditions. Qualifications: Education: Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, or a related field. Experience: Minimum of 2-4 years of experience as a Project Engineer or in a similar role within the construction industry, preferably with experience in hotel, commercial, or residential projects. Skills: Strong knowledge of construction processes, materials, and techniques. Proficiency in project management software (e.g., MS Project, AutoCAD). Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills. Ability to read and interpret construction drawings, specifications, and technical documents. Strong verbal and written communication skills. Ability to work well under pressure and meet deadlines. Certifications: Any additional certifications related to construction management or engineering would be an advantage. Why Join Us? Competitive salary and benefits package. Opportunity to work on prestigious hotel, commercial, and residential projects across Singapore. A supportive, team-oriented work environment. Career advancement opportunities and professional development. Exposure to a diverse range of projects, offering hands-on experience in all aspects of construction engineering.

title: Shipping Operations Executive
description: Shipping Operations Executive (Dry Bulk) We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Shipping Operations Executive to join a dynamic team managing a fleet of dry bulk vessels trading worldwide. The ideal candidate will have 2–3 years of experience in bulk shipping operations , preferably on the owners or operators desk , and a strong understanding of voyage execution, documentation, and post-fixture coordination. This is a hands-on role that offers exposure to end-to-end vessel operations, commercial decision-making, and direct liaison with charterers, agents, and masters. Key Responsibilities Oversee daily voyage operations for owned and time-chartered bulk carriers. Coordinate with ship masters, charterers, brokers, agents, and bunker suppliers to ensure smooth voyage execution. Monitor vessel performance, port rotations, laytime, demurrage, and operational costs. Prepare and review voyage instructions, cargo documentation, and port clearances. Track and report vessel positions, bunker consumption, and voyage results. Handle laytime calculations and assist with claims and post-fixture documentation. Support the commercial team with performance analysis and voyage optimisation. Maintain accurate operational records and ensure compliance with company and regulatory requirements. Requirements Diploma or Degree in Maritime Studies, Shipping, Logistics, or related discipline. 2–3 years of relevant experience in dry bulk operations on the owners/operators desk . Strong knowledge of voyage and time charter party terms , laytime and demurrage. Familiarity with commercial shipping systems and Microsoft Office applications. Excellent communication and coordination skills across multiple stakeholders. Ability to multitask and work under pressure in a fast-paced environment. Analytical mindset with attention to detail and a strong sense of accountability. Exposure to Supramax, Panamax, or Handymax segments. Experience with claims handling, voyage estimation tools, or bunker planning . Proactive attitude with an interest in growing into a commercial or chartering function. Location & Reporting Based in Singapore. Reports to the Operations Manager / Senior Operator .

title: AI Product Manager (Management Trainee)
description: Exciting news! We are on a growing journey and building a stellar team! At Sunnystep, we are not just building leaders – we are cultivating a dynamic team that thrives on innovation, collaboration, and collective success. Why should you be part of Sunnystep's success? We are an innovative force in consumer retail with a clear brand purpose — we believe wellness starts with our feet. Good shoes support our feet and our body, and help us move and live better. We are redefining comfort in every step to an unparalleled experience without compromising on style. Our mission is to revolutionize the footwear industry by crafting the most comfortable shoes with unwavering commitment to environmental responsibility ensuring that every step our customers take not only embodies unrivalled comfort but also leaves a positive impact on the planet. You'll operate as an AI Product Manager in training, working directly with the CEO / product lead to build, ship, and iterate AI-powered features that improve business metrics (conversion, productivity, customer satisfaction, cost reduction). This is not a "research role". You are expected to ship weekly, learn fast, and turn ambiguous problems into working product flows. What You'll Own (Core Responsibilities) AI Feature Delivery (End-to-End) Convert business problems into clear product requirements (PRDs, user stories, workflows).Build MVPs fast using LLMs + simple workflows (prompting, RAG, automation, human-in-the-loop).Partner with engineers/design to ship, measure, iterate.Own rollout, adoption, training, and feedback loops. User & Workflow Design (Practical, Not Theoretical) Conduct lightweight discovery: shadow users, interview store teams/internal stakeholders.Map workflows (before/after): time saved, error reduction, throughput increase.Define UX for AI features (approval flows, confidence display, fallback behavior). Data + Product Analytics Track and improve key metrics: adoption, usage frequency, activation rate, task success rate.Set up dashboards or lightweight measurement systems (Sheets/Looker/Lark/SQL).Use evidence to decide what to build next—no guesswork. Prompting + Evaluation Discipline Design prompts and test systematically (A/B variants, edge cases).Build evaluation sets and define “good output” standards.Reduce hallucinations and failure modes using guardrails + structured outputs. Operator Mindset (Execution + Systems) Turn messy workflows into repeatable SOPs.Create playbooks so results scale beyond you.Handle stakeholder alignment and updates with clarity and speed. Minimum Requirements (Must Have) Strong structure and writing: can produce clear PRDs and specs. High agency: takes initiative without being asked repeatedly. Comfort with ambiguity + speed: you don’t freeze when it’s unclear. Basic AI literacy:understands what LLMs can/can’t docan write prompts and test systematically knows simple concepts like RAG, embeddings, evaluation Analytical ability: can interpret data and form a crisp recommendation. Preferred Requirements (Big Advantage) Built something tangible before (app, automation, dashboard, prototype). Experience with tools like:Notion/Lark, Sheets, SQL basicsZapier/Make, Airtable, RetoolPython, API basics Prior exposure to retail, e-commerce, customer support, operations. Culture Fit (Non-Negotiables) You must naturally operate with: Extreme Ownership: results > excuses Speed: ship > talk Obsess over quality & detail: no sloppy edge cases Radical honesty: say what’s broken fast Ruthless prioritization: focus on impact, ignore noise Headquartered in Singapore, Sunnystep is committed to fostering community, inclusivity, integrity, and technology to support everybody’s journey of possibilities. We review each application thoroughly and will arrange an initial discussion with the shortlisted candidates who best meet our requirements. If you find that the requirements align with your experience and expertise, you may express your interest through this job advertisement or you may reach out to our Hiring Team – hiring@sunnystep.com .

title: Chief Technology Officer
description:
932 Design Group is looking a hands-on Chief Technology Officer (CTO) to lead our technology vision and build the foundation for scalable growth. This role is ideal for a technology leader who thrives in a fast-paced, highly regulated fintech environment, balancing strategic leadership with deep technical execution. The CTO will lead the development of secure, high-performance, and scalable platforms, ensuring technology decisions align closely with business objectives, regulatory requirements, and customer needs. Responsibilities Technology Strategy, Leadership & Execution Define and execute the company’s long-term technology and IT strategy aligned with business goals. Advise executive leadership on technology trends, risks, and opportunities. Lead digital transformation initiatives to drive innovation and competitive advantage. Establish technology governance, standards, and best practices across the organization. Lead and assemble a diverse, high-performing team of technologists/ engineers/ innovators to develop AI-driven initiatives across the organization. Product Development, Architecture & Platforms Oversee the design and delivery of secure, scalable, and high-performance platforms. Ensure system reliability, scalability, and performance while meeting business and customer requirements. Optimize application architecture, system integrations, and development processes to support rapid growth. Security, Risk Management & Compliance Own the cybersecurity and technology risk management strategy. Safeguard digital transactions, sensitive data, and mission-critical systems. Ensure compliance with financial regulations, data protection laws, and industry standards. Lead audits, regulatory reviews, disaster recovery planning, and business continuity initiatives. IT Infrastructure, Operations & DevOps Oversee cloud infrastructure, networks, and enterprise systems to ensure high availability and resilience. Drive DevOps adoption, automation, and continuous improvement to enhance efficiency and reliability. Manage IT budgets, resources, and operational performance. Team Leadership, Collaboration & Vendor Management Build, mentor, and lead high-performing engineering and IT teams. Foster a culture of innovation, accountability, and continuous improvement. Collaborate closely with business leaders, product teams, and external stakeholders. Manage third-party vendors and partners to ensure quality, performance, and value. Innovation & Technology Enablement Evaluate and adopt emerging technologies such as cloud, AI, automation, and data platforms. Enable data-driven decision-making and support customer-facing product innovation. Ensure technology solutions remain modern, flexible, and future-ready. Champion creativity and experimentation in the design and development of AI-powered solutions,
automation, and intelligent systems that enhance operational efficiency, unlock insights, and support business growth. Translate business challenges into practical, scalable AI solutions while ensuring ethical use, security, and regulatory compliance. Qualifications Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in computer science, information technology, engineering, or a related field. Proven experience as a CTO or senior technology leadership role. Strong background in building and scaling secure, cloud-based platforms. Extensive understanding of software development, IT infrastructure and cybersecurity best practices. Strong strategic thinking, problem-solving skills, communication, and stakeholder management skills. Hands-on knowledge of AI-driven creative workflows, including generative design, BIM, advanced visualization technologies, and other AI applications, applied to interior design, architecture industry. Please apply by sending in your CV and cover letter to samantha.koh@932designgroup.com.

title: Service Delivery Manager/Service Senior Manager
description: NCS is a leading technology services firm that operates across the Asia Pacific region in over 20 cities, providing consulting, digital services, technology solutions, and more. We believe in harnessing the power of technology to achieve extraordinary things, creating lasting value and impact for our communities, partners, and people. Our diverse workforce of 14,000 has delivered large-scale, mission-critical, and multi-platform projects for governments and enterprises in Singapore and the APAC region. As a Service Senior Manager, you will manage and govern application and systems operations (Application Manager Services, AMS) for NCS Telco+ SBG’s clients. The role carries primary accountability for AMS delivery excellence, while also supporting platform evolution, customer success, solution delivery, and growth initiatives. The SSM will operate as a techno-functional leader, combining strong understanding of procurement business processes with hands-on experience across different systems and SaaS platforms. The role requires the ability to manage multiple customers simultaneously, each with different maturity levels, priorities, and operating models. What will you do? Service Delivery & AMS Governance (Primary KPI) Own end-to-end AMS delivery and governance for multiple e-procurement customers. Ensure delivery against SLAs, KPIs, and contractual commitments across accounts. Lead L2 governance covering incident, problem, change, and release management. Drive operational reviews, service improvement plans, and risk mitigation. Act as the escalation point for delivery, service quality, and customer satisfaction. Multi-Customer & Stakeholder Management Manage multiple customer accounts concurrently with clear prioritization and governance. Serve as the primary delivery interface for senior stakeholders across all domains and functional areas per client setup. Balance competing priorities while ensuring consistent service quality across customers. Build trusted-advisor relationships and support long-term customer success. Onshore–Offshore Delivery Leadership Lead and coordinate delivery with offshore AMS and delivery teams across India, Philippines and China. Ensure effective planning, workload management, quality assurance, and documentation. Support capability building, mentoring, and performance management of delivery teams. Drive standardization, best practices, and continuous improvement. Solutioning, Delivery & Growth Support Support pre-sales activities including solutioning, estimations, pricing, and proposal development. We offer a competitive compensation and benefits package , as well unique-to-Booking.com benefits which include: Annual paid time off and generous paid leave scheme including: parent, grandparent, bereavement, and care leave Industry leading product discounts - up to 1400 per year - for yourself, including automatic Genius Level 3 status and Booking.com wallet credit Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (DEI) at Booking.com: Diversity, Equity & Inclusion have been a core part of our company culture since day one. A career in Ship Broking is exciting, interesting, challenging and highly competitive, not to mention rewarding. We are looking for a Senior Shipbroker to join our Tankers desk. Drive – self-motivation and the desire and commitment to succeed, deliver excellence and make positive change. Relationship building – excellent communicator to effectively build strong, long-lasting relationships with colleagues, clients and others. Collaboration – working well with others across diverse backgrounds to share information, develop skills and deliver results. Resilience - persisting and adapting to changing or difficult situations, and adopting a positive and focused approach. Smart - problem-solving and analytical abilities, with a curious and inquisitive mind, and an openness to new ideas. Other requirements Previous experience or exposure of Tankers broking preferred, with at least 10 years’ relevant experience Prior experience working in a global multinational company (MNC) is advantageous Proven track record of building strong working relationships with clients and develop new business Good commercial judgement with excellent negotiation skills Excellent communication skills (both verbal and written) Fluency in English is essential to liaise with colleagues on desk as well as clients Driven, self-motivated and client-focused An outgoing and confident personality Strong numeracy skills and excellent presentation skills Confident and able to work well under pressure Strong attention to detail Strong MS office skills in Excel, PowerPoint, Word Special Job Requirements Travel as required. Experience & Qualifications Degree in Accounting/Finance; professional qualification (CA, CPA, or ACCA) is highly preferred. 6–10 years of experience, with at least 3 years in a "lean team" environment where you were required to be both a strategist and a doer. Advanced proficiency in ERP systems (e.g., NetSuite, SAP B1, Xero) and data visualization tools. You should be someone who "breaks" and then "fixes" spreadsheets to make them better. You are comfortable with ambiguity and enjoy the challenge of building processes from scratch rather than just following existing ones. You don’t just report numbers; you interrogate them until they tell a story. Ability to explain complex financial concepts to non-finance stakeholders with clarity and empathy. Job Highlights Annual Leave - 14 days onwards Birthday Leave - We believe employee should take a day off to celebrate their birthday with their loved ones. FREE TCM services and Tech-enhanced therapies for our employees, and 50% discount for employees’ family members! Attractive and Good Commissions Entitlement Great working culture - Fun and enjoyable workplace that embraces Diversity and Work-life balance/harmony. Why Should You Join Us? GREAT CULTURE AND ATMOSPHERE: Because you will be working along with amazing and crazy fun people. We have cyclists, chefs, yogis, gamers, athletes, animal lovers, motivational and inspirational trainers, foodies, and many more. You’ll definitely love your #workfam. GREAT SALARY PACKAGE AND BENEFITS: Because we offer benefits which are most desired and valued by people. We believe that the people in the team are the backbone of the company, the core of everything we do. This is why we invest so much in our employee’s welfare and future. GROWTH AND CAREER DEVELOPMENT: Because the sky's the limit and we firmly believe that our employees' growth, development, and well-being are the most significant factor. We hope to grow with our employees, we want our people to shine brighter as they progress with the company. DIVERSITY: Because we value equity and inclusion. We are an equal opportunity employer who makes decisions without regard to age, identity, gender, race, colour, ethnicity, etc. We encourage our employees to be their true selves at work.

description: As a Senior Network Engineer, you will produce detailed network architecture designs and implement network systems to meet the data communication needs as required. documents for OPS Network and SMRC Network, firewalls configuration, IDS/IPS and hardening states. • Prepare ITD (Test procedure) document for OPS Network and SMRC Network · Prepare new or update all the relevant documents (e.g. Maintenance or User Manual, Config / Rollback / Troubleshoot Procedure) required for the OPS Network (FMC, FTD (firewalls)) and SMRC Network (OFL+ONL) Integration Support: • Support configuration changes to system platforms in factory and customer sites. • Work on the configurations of interfaces, zones, firewall policies, IPS, application filtering for single firewall (Offline) • Support the integration of Offline HSE and Offline Remote Site Switches with Offline SMRC Servers e.g. PIM/PAM, SNMS/SMS/NMS/LMS, RADIUS, NTP, SIEM etc (Offline) • Support the integration of Online AMTS New HSE & Remote sites switches with Offline SMRC Servers e.g. PIM/PAM, SNMS/SMS/LMS, RADIUS, NTP, SIEM etc. (Offline) • Assist in the SMRC network configuration, Integration, testing, verification and troubleshooting of the following technology used in SMRC Network (Online SMRC) • Work on the configuration, integration, testing, verification and troubleshooting of Online OPS Network selected firewalls / external interfaces (particularly from the Firewall application content inspection (Layer 4 through Layer 7)) (OPS Network) • Configure, optimize and test the transparent firewall failover functionality running in HA and standalone mode with online interfaces for the traffic impact (OPS Network) • Support External Support Platform (ESP) setup, including network platform configuration & Integration (Layer 2 and Layer 3) of switch, configure firewall with S2S VPN, DMZ, NAT and Internet using ACL policies, IPS policies, URL & Content filtering policies, VPN policies, NAT policies, hardening of switch and firewall as per the hardening guideline • Provide troubleshooting support during online deployment & integration (OPS Network) Hardening Support: • Assist on the hardening of Online / Offline SMRC network equipment and firewalls • Assist on the hardening of Online / offline OPS firewalls (OPS Network). • Troubleshoot / fix any operational / unknown issues occurs in the environment due to the hardening configuration on network devices (Offline, Online SMRC) • Support in case any issue with hardening of online firewalls / any operational issue occurs due to the hardening of online network devices (OPS Network) Requirements · Degree in Electrical / Electronic / Telecommunications Engineering · Minimum 5 years of experience working in the relevant field/industry · At least cat 2 security clearance · Strong advantage on following Professional qualifications: Ø Cisco Certified Networking Associate (CCNA), Cisco Certified Networking Professional (CCNP), Cisco Certified Design Associate (CCDA) Ø Huawei Certified ICT Associate (HCIA), Huawei Certified ICT Professional (HCIP) Ø Or other vendor specific courses, such as Juniper Certified Internet Expert (JNCIE) · Professional knowledge and experience in network detailed design and implementations Ø On technologies including SDH, MPLS-TP, IP, IP/MPLS, Ethernet etc, Ø Cable network design (fibre, copper, structured cabling), Ø 4G/5G/Wifi network design, Ø Infra design (Virtualisation, VMWare, Windows, Linux, IT services), Ø Cybersecurity implementation (Hardening, FW, AV,…), Ø Scripting (python, …), Ø Network detailed design, administration, troubleshooting, capacity optimization, Ø Network security design and implementation. · Demonstrating a degree of initiative, and resourcefulness in solving problems · Resourceful and analytical in design activities, ability to use pertinent data for meticulous recommendations
